Smart Coder 2.25
Smart Coder 2.25 – The Ultimate Competitive Coding Showdown in Kolkata!
Kolkata, the City of Joy, is about to turn into the City of Code!
Brace yourself for Smart Coder 2.25, a high-voltage competitive coding challenge designed to push your problem-solving skills, test your speed, and ignite your passion for algorithms and logic. Whether you’re a seasoned competitive programmer or just starting your journey into the world of DSA and algorithms, Smart Coder 2.25 is the perfect arena to prove your mettle.
This is not just another coding event – this is where logic meets lightning speed. Here, every second counts, every keystroke matters, and every bug could make the difference between victory and defeat.
Why Smart Coder 2.25 is Different?
In most competitions, you just solve problems. But here, you battle the clock, the leaderboard, and some of the sharpest minds in the country. We’re bringing you challenges that go beyond simple syntax or memorized patterns – we’re talking about real-time brain wars that will stretch your creativity, sharpen your thinking, and force you to optimize like never before.
This event is tailor-made for those who:
Thrive under pressure
Love finding elegant solutions to complex problems
Want to test themselves against competitive peers
Enjoy the thrill of live rankings and instant feedback

Name: InnovAIte AI Hackathon 2025 Grab  Internship opportunity Worth 20k+
Organizer: Proptelligence Technologies
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
InnovAIte AI Hackathon 2025 Grab  Internship opportunity Worth 20k+
About the Hackathon:
The Innovate AI Hackathon 2025 is a national-level competition challenging students, professionals, and innovators to leverage Artificial Intelligence for real-world impact. This hackathon aims to foster creativity, collaboration, and cutting-edge AI development, focusing on solutions that address pressing societal challenges.
Join us to turn your innovative ideas into impactful AI prototypes and compete for exciting cash prizes and invaluable opportunities.
Theme: AI for a Sustainable Future:
Participants are encouraged to develop AI solutions that contribute to sustainability, environmental conservation, and social good. Focus areas include, but are not limited to:
Climate Action & Environment: AI-powered solutions for monitoring environmental changes, optimizing resource usage, or promoting eco-friendly practices.
Smart Cities & Infrastructure: AI applications for energy efficiency, waste management, or sustainable urban planning.
Healthcare & Wellbeing: AI tools for improving public health outcomes, accessibility, or personalized wellness.
Education & Inclusivity: AI-driven platforms for personalized learning, skill development, or bridging digital divides.
Ethical AI: Projects focusing on fair, transparent, and unbiased AI systems.
Eligibility & Team Size:
Open to all students (undergraduate and postgraduate), professionals, researchers, and AI enthusiasts across India.
No restrictions on academic background or experience level.
Team Size: 2 to 4 members. Individual participation is not allowed.
Participants must be 18 years or older.
Important Dates:
Registration Deadline: September 15, 2025 (11:59 PM IST)
Shortlisted Teams Announcement: September 15, 2025
Grand Finale: September 22, 2025 (12-hour event)
How to Participate:
Form a Team: Gather 2 to 3 enthusiastic members who are passionate about AI.
Register Online: Complete the registration form by the deadline (September 10, 2025). Ensure all team members' details are accurate.
Submit Your Idea: Prepare and submit your Phase 1 idea proposal (PPT/document) by the specified deadline.
Await Shortlisting: Keep an eye on your email for the announcement of shortlisted teams.
Join the Grand Finale: If shortlisted, prepare to join the intensive 12-hour hackathon to build and demonstrate your prototype.

Introduction:
Pixel Wizard, the ultimate frontend web development event at techFEST ‘25, is here to challenge your creativity and coding skills. This dynamic competition brings together designers, developers, and digital creatives to create amazing, usable web projects within a time constraint.
In this edition, the focus shifts entirely to frontend development — giving you the freedom to use any tech stack of your choice. Whether it's React, Vue, plain HTML/CSS/JS, or anything in between — your imagination is the only limit.
Pixel Wizard is your chance to deploy ideas, exchange knowledge, and be part of a vibrant community where creativity meets code.
Gameplay:
The competition will run for 24 continuous hours.
A single problem statement will be given to all teams at the start.
Participants must deploy their final project and ensure Inspect Element is enabled.
The competition will be held in a hybrid format – both online and offline participation is allowed.
After submission, participants must fill out a Google Form to share team details and the deployed project link.
Each team must maintain a GitHub repository named after the team name.
Judges' decisions will be final and binding.
Rules:
This is a frontend-only contest.
Any frontend tech stack (React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, Tailwind, Bootstrap, etc.) is allowed.
Teams must have a minimum of 2 and maximum of 3 members.
Teams must register on both the techFest ‘25 website and Unstop.
Plagiarism is strictly prohibited — do not copy existing projects (e.g., Pilgrim or any public project).
If found copied, the team will be immediately disqualified.
Disqualified teams will not receive participation certificates.
Scoring Criteria:
UI/UX design, responsiveness, and functionality are key scoring parameters.
Use of proper coding practices and naming conventions will add to the score.
Deployment quality and accessibility (inspect enabled) will be considered.
Originality and creativity of the project will be rewarded.
Certification:
Top 3 teams will receive Winner Certificates and cash prizes.
All valid participants (except disqualified teams) will receive Participation Certificates.

All the participants must make payment on the techFEST'25
website
. Without payment, their participation will not be considered, and they will be disqualified.
The organizers reserve the right to modify the rules, prize pool, dates, venue, or timing of the competition if deemed necessary under unforeseen circumstances.
All guidelines are subject to change. Participants are advised to regularly check the official techFEST ‘25 website for any updates. Any changes or announcements will be posted there. Adherence to the latest rules is mandatory for all teams.

Introduction:
Get ready for a coding competition where teamwork and precision are everything. In Logic Flow, your team of three will face three interconnected coding problems. The output of one coder’s solution becomes the input for the next, forming a continuous chain.
But beware—if one coder fails, the entire chain will collapse in a cascading failure. This challenge tests your coding skills, problem-solving abilities, and your team's coordination under pressure.
Can your team rise to the challenge and complete the cascade flawlessly? Step up, sync up, and prove your coding strength!
Guidelines:
The contest will run for 1.5 hour and feature 3 problems to solve one for each teammate.
Each team must consist of exactly three members.
The output of one solution becomes the input for the next. If one solution fails, subsequent solutions will become invalid.
Team Composition:
Team size: 3 members
Team members: A, B, C
Each member works on a distinct task:
A solves Problem 1 → generates output
B uses A’s output to solve Problem 2
C uses B’s output to solve Problem 3
Rules:
Each team member may only work on their assigned sub-problem.
Tools: Only approved IDEs on platform; no external communication.
Plagiarism of code or output is forbidden. Any plagiarism will result in instant disqualification.
Presence:
All teams must be present physically on-site.
Participants must register on both the official techFEST'25 website and unstop.
Disqualified contestants will not receive a participation certificate.
The decisions of the organizers will be final and binding.
Certification:
Top 3 performers will receive winner certificates and cash prizes.
All contestants (except the disqualified ones) will receive participation certificates.

Name: AI Engineer Fresher
Organizer: Linkenite
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
AI Engineer Fresher
Modern organizations receive hundreds (sometimes thousands) of emails daily. Many of these emails are support-related (e.g., customer queries, requests, or help tickets). Manually sifting through these emails, prioritizing them, and drafting professional responses can be time-consuming and error-prone.
Your challenge is to build an AI-Powered Communication Assistant that can intelligently manage these emails end-to-end. The assistant should analyze incoming emails, prioritize them based on urgency, generate appropriate responses, and display the results on a user-friendly dashboard.
The goal is to improve efficiency, response quality, and customer satisfaction while reducing manual effort.
Core Requirements:
Email Retrieval & Filtering
Fetch all incoming emails from the user’s email account (IMAP/Gmail/Outlook APIs, etc.).
Filter emails whose subject lines contain terms like:
“Support”
“Query”
“Request”
“Help”
Extract and display relevant details for each filtered email on the dashboard:
Sender’s email address
Subject
Email body
Date/time received
Categorization & Prioritization
Each eligible email should be automatically categorized and ranked:
Sentiment Analysis: Positive / Negative / Neutral
Priority: Urgent / Not urgent (e.g., based on keywords like “immediately,” “critical,” “cannot access,” etc.)
Emails marked as urgent should appear at the top of the list for processing (implement a priority queue for email processing)
Context-Aware Auto-Responses
Use an LLM (Large Language Model) to generate draft replies for each incoming email.
Responses must:
Maintain a professional and friendly tone
Be context-aware (e.g., should use a knowledge base to answer these questions (RAG + Prompt Engineering) + respond contexually - if the customer is frustrated, acknowledge their frustration empathetically)
Include relevant details (e.g., if the email mentions a product, reference it in the reply)
Prioritized emails (urgent) should be responded to first.
Information Extraction
From each incoming email, extract key information such as:
Contact details (phone number, alternate email, etc.)
Customer requirements or requests
Sentiment indicators (positive/negative words)
Any metadata that can help support teams act faster
This information should be displayed clearly on the dashboard, alongside the raw email.
Dashboard / User Interface
A simple, clean dashboard where:
The Filtered support emails are listed along with extracted key details in a structured format
Anaytics and Stats are present - Categories (sentiment + priority), total emails recieved in last 24 hours, emails resolved, emails pending, interactive graph to display these details
AI-generated responses are displayed (and can be reviewed/edited before sending)
Technical Guidelines:
You can use any tech stack (Python/Node.js/Java + React/Next.js for frontend, etc.).
For (sentiment, summarization, response generation), you may use: OpenAI GPT models, Hugging Face models (BERT, T5, DistilBERT, etc.)
For email retrieval, use: Gmail, Custom SMTP, Outlook Graph API, or standard IMAP libraries.
For storage, a simple database (SQLite, MongoDB, or PostgreSQL) is sufficient.
Evaluation Criteria
Functionality: Accuracy: How well the Email functionalities like reading emails (based on filters) and writing emails/auto-sending of replies (based on priority). Does the assistant correctly filter, categorize, and contextually respond to emails based on priority
User Experience: Is the dashboard intuitive and contains all the information to give users the right solution.
Response Quality: How accurately the AI techniques, Retrieval-Augmented-Generation (RAG), Prompt Engineering and Context embedding, are applied to generate correct response.
Timeline:
Hackathon Duration: 4 days
Deliverables:
End-to-end working solution of AI-Powered Communication Assistant
Demonstration video of all the working features of the platform
Short self-written documentation (no AI) on architecture + approach used
Impact:
This assistant has the potential to transform customer support operations by:
Reducing manual workload
Ensuring faster, empathetic responses
Extracting actionable insights from unstructured emails
Improving customer satisfaction and retention

Cipher Chase is a 24hr Capture the Flag (CTF) competition which involves solving practical security-related challenges.
It is a fun learning experience and allows participants to explore various cybersecurity and cryptography domains.
Stages & Timeline:
Stage 1 Starts: 7th November 2025, 10:00 PM
Stage 2 Starts: 8th November 2025, 10:00 AM
End: 8th November 2025, 10:00 PM
Theme: Into the Multiverse
Theme Details:
Participants will explore different universes, each with unique characteristics, challenges, and vulnerabilities.
Every universe requires different cybersecurity skills such as reverse engineering, web exploitation, cryptography, steganography, forensics, machine learning security, and pwn challenges.
Universes and Challenges:
Quantumverse
Skills Required: Reverse Engineering, Cryptography
Pixelverse
Skills Required: Steganography, OSINT
Cyberpunk 3025
Skills Required: Web Exploitation
Mystic Realms
Skills Required: Forensics, Steganography
AI Overlord Dimension
Skills Required: Prompt Injection, ML Security
Entropy Zone
Skills Required: Miscellaneous, Pwn (binary exploitation)
Rules:
Each team can comprise a maximum of 2 members
The challenges will be released as 2 sets. At the 12 hr mark, the second set of challenges will be released
Each team will have a flag; the team needs to navigate through the challenges to figure out their flag. Upon submission of the correct flag, the team will earn points
The number of points per challenge is determined by the number of teams that have solved the challenge (dynamic scoring)
The flag for each team is unique, if a team enters the flag of another team they will be disqualified
Evaluation Criteria:
Challenge Weighting: Each challenge has a set point value (higher for harder problems)
Dynamic Scoring: The points for a challenge drop as more teams solve it, to reward early solvers
Flag Validity: A correct flag earns points instantly, no penalties for incorrect flag

Name: HerSpark Ideathon 2025
Organizer: Thakur College of Engineering and Technology (TCET), Mumbai
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
HerSpark Ideathon 2025
We are excited to invite you to
Aether
's HerSpark Ideathon 2025 (Only Females),
a unique event designed to
empower young women in STEM
by harnessing the power of technology for social good. At HerSpark, we believe in the potential of technology to transform lives and create a better world. Whether you’re passionate about tackling societal challenges, innovating for underserved communities, or exploring the intersection of technology and social impact, HerSpark offers a platform to showcase your creativity and problem-solving skills.
Our themes,
“Money skills for everyone: safer, smarter, more inclusive”
and
"Tools for online safety, digital literacy, safe communication, data privacy,"
challenge participants to use their technical expertise and creativity to develop innovative solutions that address critical social issues. This is your chance to make a meaningful impact and win exciting prizes
.
Who can participate?
Participation is open exclusively to female students enrolled in
Junior College and Diploma College from Science Streams
Undergraduate College from Science and Engineering Streams
Team Composition:
All members must be female.
Each team must consist of 2-4 members.
All team members must belong to the same educational category (e.g., Junior College/Diploma or Undergraduate/Engineering College).
Each team must nominate a Team Leader to serve as the primary point of contact.
Participants will work in teams to tackle challenges aligned with our two primary tracks
Why Participate?
Opportunity to Innovate
: Create and develop solutions that can make a tangible impact on society.
Collaborate with Peers
: Work with like-minded individuals passionate about technology and social issues.
Gain Recognition
: Showcase your skills to industry experts and gain visibility in the tech community.
Exciting Prizes
: Compete for trophies, gifts, and other exciting rewards.
Tracks:
Track 1: FinTech & Digital Literacy for Everyone
(Open to Junior College and Diploma College girl students from Commerce, Business, and Science Streams)
This track empowers young women to explore the intersection of finance and technology. Participants will delve into the world of FinTech, focusing on how digital tools and platforms can make financial services more accessible, inclusive, and secure. The goal is to equip students with the knowledge and skills to navigate digital finance safely, develop smarter money management practices, and foster financial literacy among underserved communities. By addressing the challenges of financial inclusion, this track encourages designing innovative solutions that make financial systems safer and more inclusive for everyone.
Track 2: Cyber Safety & Digital Empowerment
(Open to Undergraduate College girl students from Engineering, Computer Science, and Technology Streams)
This track challenges students to explore the essential tools and strategies for maintaining safety in the digital age. Participants will examine online safety protocols, data privacy, secure communication practices, and how to recognize and respond to cyber threats. The focus is on empowering women to become digital leaders in creating safe online environments, promoting digital literacy, and developing resources that encourage secure internet usage. By tackling issues like identity theft, online harassment, and data breaches, this track aims to prepare young women to advocate for and build safer digital spaces for all users.
Two Rounds:
Round 1 (Online):
Teams will present their project ideas and materials to the panel
Round 2 (Offline):
Offline Round  Duration
: 6 hours
Offline Round Venue
: Thakur College of Engineering and Technology, Mumbai
Attendance is mandatory for all selected participants
We would love for you and your peers to join us for this exciting event. For more information please do go through the
Guidelines PPT
attached
or you can also reach out to us at
tcet.lr@tcetmumbai.in
.
We look forward to seeing your ideas light up the path to a brighter future!

Name: MedhaManthan (Hackathon – Veda 2025)
Organizer: Aditya university surampalam
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
MedhaManthan (Hackathon – Veda 2025)
Description
Are you ready to transform your innovative ideas into reality in just a few hours? Veda 2025 proudly presents
MedhaManthan
, a high-octane, rapid-prototyping hackathon where your wisdom meets innovation head-on!
This isn't your typical overnight hackathon. MedhaManthan is a 5-hour sprint designed to challenge your creativity, coding skills, and teamwork. Whether you're a seasoned developer or just starting your coding journey, this is the perfect platform to build something amazing, learn from mentors, and showcase your talent.
Dive into one of our three exciting themes, build a working prototype, and pitch your solution to a panel of expert judges. We provide the platform; you bring the passion and the code!
Themes & Problem Statements:
To spark your imagination, we have curated three tracks. Your project must align with one of the following themes. Here are some ideas to get you started:
Quick Builds:
(Simple, yet impactful applications that solve a specific problem.)
AI-Powered Quiz Bot:
A fun chatbot that engages users with trivia on any topic.
Digital Resume Generator:
A web app that takes user details and generates a clean, professional resume, ready to be exported as a PDF.
Mood-Based Recommender:
An app that suggests music or motivational quotes based on the user's current mood.
Flashcard Maker:
A smart tool for students to auto-generate study flashcards from text.
Fun with APIs:
(Leverage the power of public APIs to create something new and exciting.)
Weather + Outfit Suggestion App:
Pulls live weather data and suggests the perfect outfit for the day.
AI Meme Generator:
An app that takes an image and text to auto-generate a hilarious meme.
Voice-to-Notes Converter:
A tool that records speech and converts it into summarized text notes.
Campus Helper Bot:
A Q&A bot to help students with campus event schedules, locations, and rules.
Social Impact
(Use technology to create solutions for real-world challenges.)
Waste Segregation Identifier:
An app that uses image recognition to identify waste as recyclable, organic, or hazardous.
Emergency Contact App:
A one-click safety app that sends an alert message with the user's live location to pre-selected contacts.
Accessible Learning Tool:
A helpful application that converts text into clear speech for visually impaired learners.
Assemble your team, pick your challenge, and get ready to build, innovate, and win!
Rules & Guidelines:
Please read the following rules carefully. All participants are expected to abide by them.
Team Formation
Team size must be between
2 to 4 members
.
Lone wolves are not allowed.
Teams can consist of students from different colleges.
Hackathon Duration & Structure
The event will last for
5 hours
.
This includes time for idea finalization, development, testing, and final presentation.
The exact schedule will be shared with registered participants.
Project Development
Originality:
Your project must be developed significantly during the hackathon. You are allowed to use pre-existing templates, open-source libraries, frameworks, and public APIs. However, submitting a project that was fully completed before the event is strictly prohibited and will result in disqualification.
Technology:
There are no restrictions on the programming languages, frameworks, or tools you can use. Choose the tech stack you are most comfortable with.
Theme Adherence:
All projects must align with one of the three themes announced:
Quick Builds, Fun with APIs, or Social Impact
.
Submission
All teams must submit their project before the final deadline. Late submissions will not be accepted.
The submission must include:
A link to your code repository (e.g., GitHub).
A short description of your project and the problem it solves.
A link to a working demo or a short presentation/video.
The submission portal link will be provided during the event.
Pitching & Presentation
Each team will be given
5-8 minutes
to present their project to the judges.
This will be followed by a
2-minute Q&A session
.
Your presentation should be clear, concise, and effectively demonstrate your project's features and value.
Code of Conduct
Collaboration:
While we encourage a collaborative environment, your team's work must be your own. Any form of plagiarism will lead to immediate disqualification.
Mentorship:
Mentors will be available to provide guidance and answer questions. However, they are not permitted to write code for any team.
Respect:
All participants must maintain a respectful and professional demeanor. Harassment, discrimination, or any offensive content in projects or behavior will not be tolerated.
Resources

Name: Rewind And Recode (Tamil Nadu)
Organizer: International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Bhubaneswar
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Rewind And Recode (Tamil Nadu)
Only Join If Your college comes inside TamilNadu State
Organiser - IIIT BHUBANESWAR & VIT Chennai
Everything you need to know about
Rewind and Recode
Rewind and Recode Hackathon — A National Arena for India’s Brightest Tech Minds
Step into the world of innovation, creativity, and non-stop coding at the Rewind and Recode Hackathon, a premier National Level Hackathon hosted by the esteemed International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Bhubaneswar. This one-of-a-kind event brings together the best and brightest problem solvers, coders, and innovators from across India to tackle real-world challenges with disruptive technology and bold ideas.
Scheduled from 7th to 9th November 2025, Rewind and Recode isn’t just another hackathon — it’s a launchpad for student innovation, a battlefield of logic and creativity, and a celebration of India’s next-generation tech talent. With 24 hours of non-stop ideation, coding, collaboration, and execution, participants will compete head-to-head to build impactful, scalable, and solution-oriented tech projects.
Whether you're from a premier institute like IIT, NIT, IIIT or a passionate developer from any engineering college across India, if you're driven to create meaningful solutions, this is your stage. This hackathon invites teams from every corner of the country, ensuring a diverse pool of minds working across multiple themes — including healthcare, sustainability, agriculture, finance, education, cybersecurity, and more.
The problem statements will be released on 7th November at 8:00 AM IST, marking the beginning of the hackathon. Teams will then engage in a high-intensity sprint — analyzing, brainstorming, coding, and pitching — all within a tightly packed 24-hour window. The atmosphere will be buzzing with energy at the on-site venue of IIIT Bhubaneswar, as students code side-by-side with peers, under the mentorship of experienced tech experts and industry leaders.
But it’s not just about writing code. Rewind and Recode emphasizes end-to-end problem solving — identifying core pain points, proposing real-world solutions, justifying tech stacks, building intuitive UIs, and pitching like professionals. It’s a full-cycle product development experience compressed into 24 hours — intense, insightful, and insanely rewarding.
Registrations open in August 2025, and interested teams are advised to register early through the official portal. Once registered, teams will be eligible for further updates, problem statements, and participation in internal shortlisting (if applicable via respective institutions). Ensure your team is formed, registered, and ready before the hackathon begins!
What’s in it for you?
Besides exciting prizes, certificates, and recognition, you get a chance to:
Network with students and tech leaders from across India
Receive mentorship from professionals
Test your skills in a real-world, time-constrained setup
Strengthen your portfolio and technical credibility
This is not just a hackathon — it’s your chance to shine on the national stage.
So, gather your team. Register this August 2025.
And from 7th to 9th November, come to IIIT Bhubaneswar to Rewind what you know… and Recode what the world needs.
Let’s innovate. Let’s hustle. Let’s Hack.
We are co-organising this with VIT chennai and our community partners are SVCE Chennai and Karunya University, Coimbatore.
Hackathon Problem Statements
(
Only for Tamil Nadu)
1
.
Rural Education Connectivity Gap
Despite digital initiatives, only 18.47% of rural schools in India have internet access compared to 47.29% in urban schools, and just 44.9% of rural schools possess basic digital infrastructure. Over 400 million rural students lack consistent connectivity for online learning.[1][2]
Problem Statement: Enable seamless connectivity and resource sharing between existing school networks, local community centers, and government e-learning portals to bridge the rural-urban digital divide in education.
2
.
Fragmented Healthcare Data Exchange
Under the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission, India’s health data ecosystem remains characterized by non-digitized records, lack of standardization, and minimal interoperability across hospitals, clinics, and labs. Clinicians cannot access unified patient histories, leading to duplicated tests and delayed care.[3]
Problem Statement: Integrate disparate healthcare systems—public and private—into a unified data exchange framework that ensures secure, standardized patient record sharing across India’s healthcare network.
3. Urban Waste Management Siloes
Indian cities generate 130,000–150,000 MT of municipal solid waste daily, with 72% of wastewater untreated, and 800 million MT of legacy waste languishing in dumpsites. Urban Local Bodies lack integrated platforms for tracking collection, processing, and citizen participation.[4][5]
Problem Statement: Connect municipal waste collection services, recycling facilities, and citizen reporting channels into a single integrated dashboard to optimize urban waste management across Indian cities.
4. Farmer Market Access and Price Discovery
Despite e-NAM covering 585 markets, only 15% of small and marginal farmers engage digitally due to literacy and infrastructure barriers. Middlemen often capture 30–40% of final sale price, leaving farmers undercompensated.[6]
Problem Statement: Enable direct market access for India’s smallholder farmers by providing an independent digital marketplace that operates offline and supports local language interactions for fair price discovery.
5. Rural Healthcare Delivery Deficit
Over 70% of India’s rural population lacks timely access to specialist care, and only 28% of government primary health centers are adequately equipped for telemedicine. Poor connectivity and limited medical personnel exacerbate rural health disparities.[3]
Problem Statement: Deliver standalone tele-health services in rural India that function without continuous internet, ensuring remote diagnosis, emergency triage, and health education despite infrastructure constraints.
6. Informal Sector Financial Exclusion
Approximately 190 million Indians work in the informal economy, yet 50% have no bank account, and 35% lack access to credit. Traditional credit scoring excludes them due to absence of formal income records.[2]
Problem Statement: Provide an independent financial identity solution for informal workers, enabling secure micro-loans and savings while operating offline and without reliance on formal banking infrastructure.
7. Last-Mile Renewable Energy Access
Despite rapid renewable capacity growth, 15% of India’s remote villages remain off-grid, relying on kerosene and diesel generators. Intermittent grid expansion and maintenance challenges persist.[7]
Problem Statement: Deploy standalone microgrid units powered by renewables that deliver reliable electricity to off-grid Indian villages, ensuring essential services and community development without grid dependence.
8. Climate-Resilient Agriculture Advisory Network
Indian farmers face crop losses up to 30% due to extreme weather, pests, and water scarcity. Government advisories are fragmented and slow, while private agritech apps lack rural reach.[8]
Problem Statement: Combine government meteorological data and private satellite imagery to deliver a hybrid advisory system for climate-resilient farming, capable of functioning online in cities and offline in remote areas.
9. Smart Traffic and Pollution Control in Tier-II Cities
Tier-II Indian cities report PM2.5 levels up to 200 µg/m³, double WHO limits, driven by uncoordinated traffic flows and outdated transport systems. Data siloes across traffic police, municipal bodies, and public transport agencies hinder effective action.[9][10]
Problem Statement: Integrate traffic sensor data, public transport schedules, and pollution monitoring stations into a unified platform that optimizes traffic signals and issues public health alerts, with a fallback standalone mode during network outages.
10. Urban-Rural Waste-to-Resource Linkage
India generates 62 million tonnes of plastic waste annually, yet recycling rates remain below 30% due to fragmented collection and processing chains. Rural areas often lack collection services, leading to environmental and health hazards.[11][12]
Problem Statement: Build a hybrid system that links rural waste collection units with urban recycling centers through both digital coordination and offline courier networks, ensuring continuous material flow and minimizing environmental impact.
11. Real-Time Multimodal Medical Imaging Diagnostic System
The Problem: Surgical and diagnostic workflows suffer from delays and missed anomalies due to reliance on manual image interpretation. Studies show up to 30% of critical surgical complications could be prevented with better intraoperative imaging alerts. Moreover, a Lancet Oncology review reports that subtle tissue deformations and instrument misalignments are missed in 15–20% of cases without automated support.
Problem Statement: Engineer a highly complex, multi-modal, real-time diagnostic software system that ingests live 2D/3D volumetric data from CT, MRI, or ultrasound to perform semantic segmentation and detect minute anomalies. The system must trigger contextual LLM alerts with prioritized differential diagnoses for clinicians.
12. AI-Powered Artisans’ Marketplace Assistant
The Problem: Local artisans in India generate over ₹70,000 crore in annual revenue but lack digital reach. NITI Aayog reports that only 8% of artisans sell online, losing access to global markets and fair pricing. Traditional marketplaces fail to capture artisans’ cultural narratives, limiting customer engagement.
Problem Statement: Build an AI-powered marketplace assistant that automates cataloging, enriches storytelling, and provides smart pricing and marketing guidance to empower local artisans while preserving cultural authenticity.
13. Confidential Empathetic Youth Mental Wellness Assistant
The Problem: India’s National Mental Health Survey 2015–16 found 10.6% of youth aged 13–17 have diagnosable mental health conditions, with suicide as the leading cause of death among 15–29 year-olds. Mental health services are scarce in rural areas, and stigma prevents help-seeking.
Problem Statement: Design an AI-powered, confidential, and empathetic mental wellness assistant for youth that provides conversational support, personalized coping strategies, crisis detection, and culturally sensitive adaptations.
14. AI-Powered Early Warning Network for Infectious Disease Spillover
The Problem: Emerging infectious diseases primarily arise from zoonotic spillovers, where pathogens transfer from animals to humans. Over 70% of new infectious diseases are of animal origin, posing a serious global health threat. Traditional surveillance systems fail to detect early spillover signs due to fragmented data from wildlife monitoring, health care centers, environmental changes, and community reports. The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the urgent need for rapid, integrated surveillance to identify zoonotic outbreaks before wide spread.
Problem Statement: Design a global AI system that continuously ingests live data from wildlife surveillance, hospital emergency reports, social media signals, and environmental sensors to detect and flag early signs of zoonotic spillovers. The system must identify transmission pathways and generate real-time, accessible alerts tailored to varying regional healthcare systems and languages.
15. Real-Time Climate-Adaptive Water Distribution Platform
The Problem: India faces severe water scarcity and flooding due to climate variability, impacting urban and agricultural water supply. The Central Water Commission reports frequent mismatches between water availability and demand, resulting in acute shortages or flooding events in various regions. Existing water management systems lack real-time integration of hydrological data and community alerts, limiting adaptive resource allocation.
Problem Statement: Build an AI-driven platform that fuses real-time hydrological data with demand forecasts to optimize water allocation dynamically in urban and rural sectors. The platform must predict shortages or flooding, enact conservation/redirection protocols, and issue culturally sensitive alerts to utilities and communities.
16. AI-Assisted Real-Time Disability Navigation Companion
The Problem: Approximately 26.8 million people with disabilities live in India, many facing significant mobility and accessibility challenges [Census India Report 2011]. Public transport and urban infrastructure often lack real-time adaptive support for people with mobility, sensory, or cognitive impairments. Current navigation aids are limited or require continuous connectivity, which is sparse in remote or complex environments.
Problem Statement: Develop a multi-modal AI navigation companion that utilizes live wearable and environmental sensor data, along with public transport APIs, to offer real-time hazard detection, adaptive routing, and emotional support in users’ native languages. The system should function offline with strict privacy safeguards.
17. Real-Time Agricultural Supply Chain Integrity Monitor
The Problem: India’s agricultural supply chains are plagued by spoilage, fraud, and unregulated pesticide use. The Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) reports up to 40% post-harvest losses in perishable produce due to inadequate cold chains and mislabeling. Consumers and regulators lack access to transparent, real-time supply chain data to ensure food safety and authenticity.
Problem Statement: Engineer an AI platform that monitors perishable produce from farm to market using live IoT data, satellite images, and crowd inputs to detect spoilage, fraudulent relabeling, and pesticide misuse in real-time. The system must deliver compliance alerts, propose corrective actions, and provide transparent tr
aceability that supports farmers and consumers alike.

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Aether HackConquest Hackathon
We are thrilled to invite you to Aether's HackConquest 2025, a two-round online hackathon designed to challenge students to innovate, collaborate, and develop impactful solutions for real-world problems. HackConquest provides a platform to showcase your technical expertise, creativity, and problem-solving skills. Whether you are passionate about building web apps, developing sustainable solutions, or exploring cybersecurity challenges, this is your opportunity to make your ideas come to life.
Who Can Participate?
Open to undergraduate students from all colleges.
Teams must have 2 - 4 members, with at least one female member.
Multiple teams from the same college are welcome.
Themes/Problem Areas:
Participants can choose from the following focus areas:
Web & App Development  -
Design and build interactive websites or mobile applications, focusing on usability, functionality, and real-world utility.
Data Science & Analytics – Dashboards -
Analyze datasets, create insightful dashboards, and generate actionable insights for decision-making.
AI/ML & Deep Learning -
Build intelligent models, predictive algorithms, or deep learning applications to solve complex problems.
UI/UX & Frontend Design -
Craft engaging user interfaces, seamless experiences, and visually appealing designs for digital platforms.
Data Privacy, Cybersecurity & Cloud -
Develop secure systems, ensure data privacy, and explore cloud-based solutions for modern challenges.
Why Participate?
Opportunity to Innovate: Showcase your ideas and build solutions that can make a tangible impact.
Collaborate with Peers: Work with like-minded students to solve real-world problems.
Gain Recognition: Present your ideas to experienced judges and gain visibility in the tech community.
Exciting Awards: Compete for trophies, medals, certificates, and the Best Innovative Solution Award.
Platform:
HackConquest 2025 will be conducted fully online via Google Meet. Participants will receive the access link via email after registration.
Round 1 – Idea Submission & Screening (06 Sep – 20 Sep 2025):
Submissions will be made through the Unstop platform. Shortlisted teams will be notified via Unstop and email.
Submit a 2–3 page abstract (PDF/PPT) covering your chosen problem statement, solution summary (max 300 words), planned technical stack, feasibility, and expected impact.
Optional links to GitHub repos, demo videos, or prototypes can be added.
Evaluation Criteria: Innovation & Creativity (40%), Relevance (25%), Feasibility (20%), Technical Implementation Potential (15%).
Top teams will be shortlisted for the Online Hackathon Finale.

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
One day Innovating for SDG Acceleration 2030
The Internal Hackathon 2025 aims to inspire students to develop innovative, scalable solutions that accelerate progress towards the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Leveraging recent technology trends, including AI and machine learning, IoT-based smart systems, blockchain for transparency, renewable energy innovations, geospatial monitoring, and biotechnology, participants will work in cross-department teams to address urgent global challenges. This event promotes hands-on problem-solving, interdisciplinary collaboration, and the application of cutting-edge tools to deliver impactful, real-world outcomes.
Objectives:
Foster interdisciplinary teamwork to address complex SDG challenges.
Encourage innovative engineering and technological solutions with real world impact.
Raise awareness of critical SDG gaps highlighted in the 2024 UN report.
Provide a platform for rapid prototyping and practical problem solving.
Recognize and reward impactful ideas that can be scaled for societal benefit.
Team Formation:
Participants can register individually or in teams of 2–4 members.
Cross-institutional teams are allowed.
One member must be designated as the Team Leader.
Eligibility:
Open to undergraduate and postgraduate students.
Only E.G.S. Pillay Engineering College (Autonomous), Nagapattinam is allowed to participate.
No restriction on branch/discipline, but solutions must align with SDG themes.
Idea Submission:
Submissions must be the original work of the team.
Plagiarism or previously submitted projects will lead to disqualification.
Each team can submit only one idea.

Feasibility & Implementation – 30%
Alignment with SDGs – 20%
Impact & Scalability – 20%
Conduct:
All decisions by the jury/organizers will be final and binding.
Participants must maintain professional decorum throughout.
Use of AI tools is allowed, but originality and clarity of thought will be judged.
Prizes:
Certificates will be awarded to all participants.
Cash prizes/goodies for top winners (details in Prize Section).
Round 1 – Idea Submission:

Deadline: [insert date].

Submissions are reviewed by the jury panel.

Followed by 3–5 minutes Q&A from the jury.
Top teams will be declared winners.

Name: Bioinformatics - ML Challenge
Organizer: Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Nagpur
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Bioinformatics - ML Challenge
Event Overview:
The Bioinformatics & ML Hackathon is a national-level, interdisciplinary challenge that blends biology, coding, and machine learning into an immersive, hands-on experience. Open to all students, whether you’re a seasoned coder, a biology enthusiast, or someone just curious, this event invites participants to dive into bioinformatics datasets, solve real-world problems, and build innovative ML solutions that push the boundaries of biology and technology. The hackathon isn’t just a competition, it’s a chance to learn, collaborate, and create future-ready bioinformatics breakthroughs and networking.
Is This for You?
Tired of hackathons that only focus on LLM wrappers?
Bored with the same old model-building, no fresh formats or innovation in ML?
You’ve come to the right place.
What We’re Looking For:
Teams with strong analytical thinking, curiosity, and a passion for exploring new fields.
A willingness to learn bioinformatics concepts and ML techniques while competing.
Problem statements that tackle biological data challenges using creative ML approaches.
Innovation, clarity, and readiness to experiment with ideas, models, and algorithms.
Solutions that demonstrate practical applications in gene clustering, protein classification, disease prediction, and more.
Impactful, scalable, and scientifically grounded solutions that advance the field.
Event Structure:
Round 1: Online Quiz
Description: MCQ-based quiz testing biology, maths, stats, and CS basics.
Evaluation: Auto-graded, top teams qualify.
Note: Shortlisted teams will be notified through emails.
Round 2: Live Challenge Battle (Offline)
Description:
Teams compete on organizer + peer-broadcasted problems with real-time leaderboard.
Flow:
Teams solve problems given by organizers and other teams in real time through the competition portal. Each team starts with base points. They earn points for correct answers and may lose points for wrong or unanswered challenges. Teams can “attack” by sending out problems and “defend” by solving problems sent to them.
Evaluation:
Points for correct solves, penalties for unsolved broadcasts.
Teams implement ML models on real bioinformatics datasets, then present to judges.
Teams tackle real-world ML problems in bioinformatics such as gene clustering, protein classification, or cancer prediction.
Eligibility Criteria & Participation Details:
Open to all students from any discipline in India.
Teams must have 1–4 members.
+10 bonus points if the team has at least one female member.
Cross-college/cross-specialization teams allowed.
A student can join only one team.
Submissions after the deadline will not be considered.
General Rules & Guidelines:
Organizers hold full authority on rules and decorum.
Participants must follow the supervision team’s instructions.
Plagiarism or invalid broadcasts = disqualification.
Judges’ decisions are final.
Note: Examples of problem statements shared are only for reference. The actual level of difficulty and final problem statements will be disclosed on the event day.

Name: IndustrySolve Idea-Thon & Product-a-Thon 2025
Organizer: Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Delhi
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
IndustrySolve Idea-Thon & Product-a-Thon 2025
What is This Event?
IndustrySolve 2025 is a
Two-phase Innovation Competition
where you'll solve actual problems that real companies face every day.
Phase 1
:
Come up with Brilliant Ideas during our
Idea-Thon
(One Full-Day Event).
Problem statements are live now at
this Google Document!
Phase 2
:
Build your Idea into a Working Product during our
Product-a-Thon
(One Month Development with Mentorship & Support).
Think of it like this, we give you Real Problems that Companies need solved. You propose Solutions. Then you actually build those Solutions and compete for Cash Prizes and Funding Opportunities!
Why Should You Join?
Real Problems
: Work on Actual Industry Challenges, not made-up Assignments
Cash Prizes
: Win up to ₹80,000 in Total (all in cash prize money)
Free Training
: Get ₹25,000 worth of Vouchers for Professional Courses at IIIT Delhi.
Mentorship
: Learn from Industry Experts and Professors
Startup Opportunities
: Get direct access to Incubators and Funding
Build Your Portfolio
: Create something real that you can show to Future Employers
Complete Event Timeline:
Phase 1: Idea-Thon (Offline Event)
Date
: Saturday, September 13, 2025
Time
: 9:30 AM to 6:00 PM
Location
: IIIT-Delhi Campus
What You'll Do:
Choose one Problem from our List of Real Industry Problems.
Spend the whole week brainstorming your Solution Idea, prior to the Event.
Work with Mentors who will guide you to refine your idea on the Event day.
Create a Presentation and a Short Video explaining your proposed Solution.
What You Need to Submit:
Presentation (PPT)
— Explaining your Solution.
Video (2-3 minutes)
— Pitching your Idea.
Your presentation should cover:
What Problem are you solving, and why does it matter?
Your Proposed Solution and what makes it Special
How you plan to build it (Technical Details).
Why companies would want this Solution (Market relevance)
Your step-by-step plan to create a working Prototype
Result
:
Top 40 teams move to Phase 2
Online Review Meetings (Mandatory Attendance)
These are check-in meetings to make sure you're making progress:
Review 1
: September 20, 2025 - Show your initial design and plan (Top 40 teams).
Review 2
: September 27, 2025 - Show early prototype or testing results
Review 3
: October 4, 2025 - Show nearly complete prototype (Top 20 teams selected)
Missing any review meeting = Automatic Disqualification!
Phase 2: Product-a-Thon (Offline Showcase):
Date
: Saturday, October 12, 2025
Time
: 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM
Location
: IIIT-Delhi Campus
Participants
: Top 20 teams
What You'll Do:
Present your working Prototype or Proof-of-Concept
Demonstrate how your solution actually works
Compete for the top 3 positions
What You Need to Submit:
Working Prototype
(Something that actually Works!)
Updated Presentation
(Showing how you built your Idea)
Technical Documentation
(Code, Design Files, Component List, Circuit Diagrams)
Demo Video
showing your Prototype in Action
Sample Problem Statement:
Here's an example of the type of problems you'll work on:
How might we design an
IoT-enabled smart water monitoring solution
that not only tracks consumption (leaks, usage patterns, supply consistency) but also measures water quality (TDS, turbidity, contamination), and then transforms this data into
actionable insights
for households, water utilities, and suppliers (e.g., RO service providers, bottled water brands), enabling better
operational alerts, tactical planning, and strategic decision-making
?
This means you could build a smart device that tells families when their water quality is bad, alerts water companies about leaks, or helps service providers know when to visit customers.
Prizes and Rewards:
Cash Prizes (Total ₹80,000)
1st Place
: ₹40,000
2nd Place
: ₹25,000
3rd Place
: ₹15,000
Educational Vouchers:
Top 10 teams
(40 students) get ₹25,000 vouchers each, for IIIT Delhi's Professional Development Program, where you can spend an immersive semester at IIITD in the iPD-CP program - an intensive program designed and delivered largely by industry professionals to take your idea to an industry-ready prototype.
Startup Opportunities:
Promising teams get direct access to funding and business support through:
IIIT-Delhi Innovation & Incubation Centre
Electropreneur Park (Government of India)
IHFC, IIT Delhi
iHub Anubhuti, IIIT-Delhi
This means if your idea is really good, these organisations might help you turn it into a real business!
Who Can Participate?
Team Requirements
Team Size
: 2 to 4 members
Student Status
: Only current students can participate (college ID required)
Team members can be from the same or different colleges/universities (cross-college teams allowed).
What You Need to Know:
Basic understanding of Technology (NO need to be an Expert!)
Willingness to Learn and Work Hard
Interest in solving Real-world Problems
Ability to attend all Offline events and Online review meetings
Rules and Guidelines:
What's Allowed
Using Online Resources for Research
Getting help from Mentors during the Event
Using AI tools for Writing Presentations or Documentation (but you must mention this)
Working with your Team members to Develop Ideas
What's NOT Allowed
Copying someone else's Idea or Solution
Using AI to generate your Main Idea or Product Design
Having someone outside your team do the Work for you
Missing Review Meetings (automatic disqualification)
Submitting Late (automatic disqualification)
Important Requirements:
Your solution must combine
Hardware
(physical components),
Software
(apps/programs), and
Product design
(how it looks and works)
All work must be Original and created by your team
You must attend ALL review meetings
All submissions must be on time
What We Provide:
During Idea-Thon
Problem statements from Real industry Partners
Expert mentors to guide you
Workshop Space and Basic Materials
Wi-Fi and Refreshments
Guidance on Presentation and Video Creation
During the Development Phase
Online mentoring sessions
Technical support through review meetings
Resource recommendations
Feedback on your progress
During Product-a-Thon
Showcase space for demonstrations
Technical equipment for presentations
Panel of expert judges
Networking opportunities
Technical Resources Provided (For Round 2):
Participants will receive structured support at every stage of the competition. This includes access to laboratory facilities, prototyping tools, hardware components, and dedicated mentorship sessions covering design thinking, STM32 development, and rapid prototyping methodologies.
Laboratory Access:
Fully equipped workbenches with professional-grade prototyping tools.
Hardware Components:
STM32, ESP32, Arduino development kits, sensors, actuators, and electronic components (subject to availability).
Professional Equipment:
Soldering stations, multimeters, and specialised testing equipment.
Mentorship Sessions:
Expert guidance on design thinking methodologies, STM32 development, and rapid prototyping techniques.
Networking Infrastructure:
High-speed Wi-Fi and cloud development access.
Evaluation Criteria:
Innovation and Originality (25%)
: Creativity and uniqueness of the proposed solution.
Technical Feasibility (25%)
: Realistic implementation plan and technical soundness.
Industry Relevance and Impact (20%)
: Direct applicability to real-world challenges.
Prototype Development (20%)
: Quality of demonstration and proof-of-concept execution.
Presentation Clarity (10%)
: Effectiveness of communication and documentation.
Important Terms and Conditions:
Intellectual Property
: Teams retain full ownership of their intellectual property. Organisers request non-exclusive rights for promotional and academic showcase purposes only.
Artificial Intelligence Usage
: AI assistance is permitted for documentation and coding support, provided such use is fully disclosed. Completely AI-generated solutions or presentations are prohibited and will be treated as academic misconduct.
Contact Information
Organised by
: ECE Department, IIIT-Delhi
In collaboration with
: Centre for Intelligent Product Development (CiPD), IIIT-Delhi
Email
:
cipd@iiitd.ac.in
Phone
: 98217-46831 (Mr. Aryan Mansoori), 98185-29954 (Mr. Aryan Jain)
Important Dates Summary
Registration Deadline
: September 11, 2025
Idea-Thon
: September 13, 2025
Product-a-Thon
: October 12, 2025
Frequently Asked Questions:
Q: Do I need to know advanced programming?
A: No! Basic technical knowledge is enough. You'll learn and get help during the event.
Q: Do I need to bring my own materials?
A: We'll provide basic materials for the Idea-Thon. For the development phase, we will arrange some components for you.
Q: Can I work on my project after the development month ends?
A: The competition judging is based on what you complete by October 12th, but you can continue developing your idea afterwards!
Q: What happens if I win?
A: You get cash prizes, professional development vouchers, and potential access to startup incubation programs!
Ready to solve real problems and build the future? Register now and start your journey from idea to product!
This is your chance to work on something that matters, learn from experts, win significant prizes, and potentially start your own company. Don't miss out!

Name: Kyberastra 2025
Organizer: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham (AVV), Chennai
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Kyberastra 2025
Kyberastra 2025 is a 24-hour national-level hackathon organized by the School of Computing, Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Chennai, under the banner of Amrita CyberNation (ACN).
This hackathon challenges the brightest innovators from across India to tackle real-world cybersecurity and technology challenges that shape the future of digital resilience. Participants will get the chance to:
Build impactful solutions in cutting-edge domains like AI-driven cyber defence, data privacy, blockchain, resilient supply chains, and agentic AI.
Collaborate across colleges & disciplines to solve pressing problems.
Network with industry experts, mentors, and recruiters while competing for exciting prizes.
Round 1: Online Screening
Teams submit their idea & proposed solution virtually
Submission Deadline: 12th September 2025
Shortlisting: Top 15 teams announced on 13th September 2025.
Round 2: On-Campus Hackathon (24 Hours)
Date: 15th September 2025, Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Chennai Campus.
Selected teams develop working prototypes within 24 hours.
Final Presentations & Judging
Date: 16th September 2025
Teams pitch their solutions before an expert jury.
Winners announced on the same day.
Participants can choose problem statements from five futuristic domains:
Next-Gen Threat Detection & Response – AI/ML-driven real-time cyber defense.
Data Privacy, Encryption & Secure Access – Secure data protection in cloud & remote work.
Blockchain-Powered Trust & Transparency – Decentralized & tamper-proof ecosystems.
Resilient Digital Supply Chains – Secure, disruption-proof digital logistics.
Agentic AI – Adaptive AI agents for autonomous defenses.
Eligibility:
Open to all UG students from any discipline across India.
Team size: 2–5 members.
Rewards & Perks:
Exciting prize pool of 30K
Certificates for all finalists.
Chance to interact with industry leaders, mentors, and recruiters.
National-level recognition for winners & top teams.
Rules & Guidelines:
Round 1:
Teams must clearly explain the idea, problem statement, and proposed solution.
Round 2:
Teams must develop and demo a working prototype.
Original Work – Submissions must be original; plagiarism or unauthorized use of pre-built solutions leads to disqualification
Team Size – 2–5 members per team. Once registered, teams cannot be changed.
Open-source tools/libraries allowed with proper credits.
Code of Conduct – Teams must maintain professionalism, respect judges/mentors, and uphold fairness.
Decision Finality – Jury & organizers’ decisions will be final.
Judging Criteria – Evaluation will be based on:
Innovation, Feasibility, Technical depth, Presentation quality, Real-world impact.
What to Bring for Onsite Round:
Laptops, chargers, power banks.
College ID cards.
Stationery for brainstorming.
Any special tools/hardware required for your solution.
Registration Details
Fee: ₹100 per participant of a team.
Deadline for Round 1 Submissions: 12th September 2025.
Venue: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Chennai Campus.
For queries, contact:
Soumika: +91 91213 48654
Avinash: +91 93983 36648

Round 1:
Online Idea Submission Guidelines
Please note that a working prototype or functional code is not required for this preliminary round.
Presentation Content Requirements:
1.
Team Information:
Team Name
College Name
Team Leader Details (Full Name, Roll No.)
All Team Members' Details (Full Name, Roll No.)
2.
Problem Definition:
Chosen Theme: Clearly state which one of the five official hackathon themes your project addresses.
Problem Statement: A concise and clear description of the specific real-world problem you aim to solve.
3.
Proposed Solution:
Solution Overview: A detailed explanation of your innovative solution. Describe how it works and what makes it unique.
Key Features: List the main features and functionalities of your proposed project.
4.
Technical & Business Plan:
Technology Stack: Mention the primary languages, frameworks, hardware, or technologies you plan to use during implementation.
Cost/Expenses: A brief and estimated overview of any potential costs required to build or deploy the solution.
Sustainability: Explain how the project could be maintained, scaled, or sustained after the hackathon.

We suggest keeping your presentation concise and impactful, ideally between 5 to 8 slides.

Creativity, theme alignment, gameplay, storytelling, and technical execution.

1–5 members
allowed.

event page

Analytica
Event Overview
Analytica is IIIT Nagpur’s flagship Machine Learning and Data Science hackathon.
Round 1 will be hosted online on Unstop; the final offline round will be conducted at IIIT Nagpur.
Designed to ignite creativity, problem-solving, and technical rigor.
Challenges participants to apply ML/DS skills to solve real-world problems in a limited time.
What We’re Looking For
Handle datasets effectively (cleaning, preprocessing, visualization).
Apply ML/DL techniques to classification challenges.
Balance creativity with accuracy while preventing overfitting.
Demonstrate strong reasoning, clarity, and originality in solutions.
Event Structure
Round 1 – Preliminary Quiz (Online on Unstop)
Format: Online quiz.
Focus: Basic programming, ML/DS fundamentals, probability, statistics, and awareness of current tech trends.
Rules:
Only the team leader attempts the quiz.
Time-limited, auto-evaluated questions.
Top performers qualify for the final round.
Round 2 – Offline Hackathon (IIIT Nagpur Campus)
Format: 12-hour coding competition.
Theme: Advanced Machine Learning & Data Classification.
Problem Statement: Dataset related to Real-life challenges.
Tasks:
Preprocessing & exploratory data analysis (EDA).
Build classification models.
Ensure generalization (avoid overfitting).
Present results effectively.
Deliverables:
Code submission via GitHub repo + Google form.
PowerPoint presentation (problem understanding, methodology, results, challenges).
Bonus Challenge: Surprise tasks (e.g., rapid-fire quizzes) for extra points.
Evaluation Rules & Guidelines
Judging Criteria
:
Model accuracy on hidden test set.
Quality of preprocessing & EDA.
Avoiding overfitting (train vs test consistency).
Innovation in approach/feature engineering.
Clarity & depth of final submission.
Disqualification Criteria
:
Plagiarism or pre-built solutions.
Posting/sharing solutions before event ends.
Use of prohibited/pre-trained models if restricted.
Misconduct or violation of event rules.
Eligibility Criteria
Open to all students (above 15 years) across disciplines and colleges in India.
Cross-college and cross-specialization teams allowed.
Max team size: 4.
One student can only be part of one team.
Final Round Requirements
GitHub Repository
Public repo for storing and managing project code.
Optional README with description, setup, and usage instructions.
All submissions (code + documentation) must be committed before deadline.
PowerPoint Presentation
Clear, concise, and structured PPT.
Must cover:
Problem statement & approach.
Key methods and techniques used.
Demonstrations (graphs, outputs, screenshots).
General Rules & Guidelines
Issues must be reported to supervisors.
Event will be monitored by supervision team.
Judges’ decisions are final.
Offensive/inappropriate team names will be disqualified.
Use of prohibited tools/sites is forbidden.
Official Discord link will be provided for updates.
No posting solutions/write-ups before event concludes.
Participants must bring their own laptops/setups.

About the Event:
Rewind and Recode Hackathon — A National Arena for India’s Brightest Tech Minds
Step into the world of innovation, creativity, and non-stop coding at the Rewind and Recode Hackathon, a premier National Level Hackathon hosted by the esteemed International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Bhubaneswar.
This one-of-a-kind event brings together the best and brightest problem solvers, coders, and innovators from across India to tackle real-world challenges with disruptive technology and bold ideas.
Scheduled from 7th to 9th November 2025, Rewind and Recode isn’t just another hackathon — it’s a launchpad for student innovation, a battlefield of logic and creativity, and a celebration of India’s next-generation tech talent.
With 24 hours of non-stop ideation, coding, collaboration, and execution, participants will compete head-to-head to build impactful, scalable, and solution-oriented tech projects.
Whether you're from a premier institute like IIT, NIT, IIIT or a passionate developer from any engineering college across India, if you're driven to create meaningful solutions, this is your stage.
This hackathon invites teams from every corner of the country, ensuring a diverse pool of minds working across multiple themes — including healthcare, sustainability, agriculture, finance, education, cybersecurity, and more.
The problem statements will be released on 7th November at 8:00 AM IST, marking the beginning of the hackathon.
Teams will then engage in a high-intensity sprint — analyzing, brainstorming, coding, and pitching — all within a tightly packed 24-hour window.
The atmosphere will be buzzing with energy at the on-site venue of IIIT Bhubaneswar, as students code side-by-side with peers, under the mentorship of experienced tech experts and industry leaders.
Rewind and Recode emphasizes end-to-end problem solving — identifying core pain points, proposing real-world solutions, justifying tech stacks, building intuitive UIs, and pitching like professionals.
It’s a full-cycle product development experience compressed into 24 hours — intense, insightful, and insanely rewarding.

Test your skills in a real-world, time-constrained setup
Strengthen your portfolio and technical credibility
This is not just a hackathon — it’s your chance to shine on the national stage.
Call to Action:
Gather your team. Register this September 2025.
From 7th to 9th November, come to IIIT Bhubaneswar to Rewind what you know… and Recode what the world needs.
Let’s innovate. Let’s hustle. Let’s Hack.

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Srijan : The Tech Hackathon
About the Event:
Mission LiFE (Lifestyle for Environment) seeks to translate the vision of LiFE into measurable impact.
It is designed with the objective to mobilise at least one billion Indians and other global citizens to take individual and collective action for protecting and conserving the environment in the period 2022–28.
It works in three reinforcing shifts:
Change in demand, where individuals and communities adopt simple, effective green choices
Change in supply, where industry and service providers respond with cleaner products and services
Change in policy, where governments lock in these gains through supportive standards and programs
Aligned with the Government of India’s Mission LiFE initiative, this hackathon provides a platform for innovators to design innovative, practicable, and scalable solutions that promote sustainable living in the 7 thematic areas.
Themes:
Save Energy
Save Water
Reduce Waste
Reduce E-waste
Say No to Single-Use Plastic
Adopt Sustainable Food Systems
Adopt Healthy Lifestyles
Problem Statement:
Participants have to choose one theme from Mission LiFE and identify a problem, gap, or opportunity aligned to that theme and propose a technical solution (software, hardware, or hybrid) that is innovative, feasible, and impactful.
Stages of Event:
Round 1 – Idea Submission
Teams answer 3 guiding questions about their chosen problem and opportunity.
Round 2 – PPT Submission
A structured deck of 6 slides (excluding Intro, Thank You and Annexure) covering problem, solution, feasibility, and impact.
Round 3 – Final Presentation
Top 10 teams present their solution before a jury at the institute.
Evaluation Criteria:
Problem-Solving
Design-Thinking
Innovation
Prototyping
Feasibility

Multiple Teams per College: Allowed
Event Mode: Rounds 1 & 2 online; Final Round would be offline on campus
Originality: Work must be original; plagiarism or copied products lead to disqualification
Note: Food & Accommodation would be provided by the host Institute.

Business Knowledge

Name: ASTRA 2025 Hackathon – Where Curiosity Meets Cosmic
Organizer: Delhi Technological University (DTU), New Delhi
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
ASTRA 2025 Hackathon – Where Curiosity Meets Cosmic
Delhi Technological University
ASTRA Hackathon 2025 – Where Curiosity Meets Cosmic
About the Event:
The
ASTRA 2025 Hackathon
is the flagship competition of
ASTRA: Where Curiosity Meets Cosmic
, DTU’s first-ever SpaceTech & Astronomy Festival. This hackathon invites students to ideate, design, and prototype solutions to some of the most real-world challenges in space technology, astronomy, and exploration.
Participants will work in teams to solve problem statements inspired by ISRO, NASA, ESA, and future mission needs, ranging from secure satellite communications to exoplanet data analysis and lunar exploration tools.
This is your chance to
innovate at the intersection of science, technology, and imagination
, and contribute towards the future of space missions.
Event Details
Date:
September 17, 2025
Mode:
Online (submission-based for Round 1; demo & video for Round 2)
Duration:
8-Hour Hackathon Sprint (final round)
Venue:
Delhi Technological University (DTU)
Team Size:
2–4 Members
Eligibility:
Open to all college students across India
Hackathon Structure
Round 1
– Idea & Concept Submission
Teams submit
PPT or PDF
detailing their concept, methodology, datasets, and expected outcomes.
Evaluated on
innovation, feasibility, clarity, and technical approach
.
Round 2
– Demo & Video Presentation
Shortlisted teams present a
2-minute demo/video
, along with a personal interaction/online meeting.
Deliverables:
code, simulation models, dashboards, or conceptual frameworks
depending on the problem statement.
Themes & Problem Statements
Theme A – Space Communications, Safety & Operations
Quantum-secured communication for space links
Real-time collision avoidance for satellites
Satellite health anomaly detection & dashboards
Space debris tracking & visualization
Crew health monitoring (Vyommitra-inspired)
Theme B – Planetary Science, Exploration & Resource Utilization
Image enhancement for lunar shadowed regions
Exoplanet atmosphere ML models (ESA Ariel mission)
Lunar night survival & energy regulation
Ice detection on Moon/Mars using remote sensing data
Full problem statement details will be provided via email and at the event kickoff.
Judging Criteria
Solutions will be evaluated based on:
Innovation & Creativity
Technical Feasibility
Scalability & Practicality
Clarity of Demonstration & Presentation
Prizes
Total Prize:
₹10,000 Cash
Additional appreciation for
most mission-ready solution
and
best use of data
.
Why Participate?
Solve real-world challenges inspired by space agencies
Network with researchers, innovators & mentors
Hands-on exposure to space data, satellite operations & AI applications
Be part of DTU’s first-ever SpaceTech Festival
Join ASTRA 2025 Hackathon and ignite your imagination for the future of space exploration!

Name: Vibe Coding Challenge — Inter-College Edition
Organizer: BM Institute of Engineering & Technology
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Vibe Coding Challenge — Inter-College Edition
Vibe Coding — Inter-College Edition (Online → On-Campus Finale) is a friendly, beginner-first coding fest for UG students across colleges. You’ll clear a short online qualifier, complete a small focused build from home, and—if shortlisted—demo your work live at B. M. Institute of Engineering and Technology (BMIET), Sonepat, Delhi-NCR.
Why join:
Beginner-first: we value clarity over complexity, good naming, and simple, working results.
Choose your path: Web (HTML/CSS/JS), Python (Beginner), or AI & Prompts.
Learn by doing: tiny briefs, mentor tips, and practical feedback.
Recognition: certificates for valid submissions; Winner, Runner-Up, Honourable Mentions.
How it works — 3 stages
Stage-1 · Online Qualifier (this listing on Unstop)
A timed quiz that checks fundamentals + a micro “thinking” response.
Format: MCQs (web/python/prompting basics) .
Duration: 30 minutes; single sitting; shuffled questions/options.
Scoring : MCQs 75.
Output: Top entries are shortlisted for Stage-2. Shortlisting is per track.
Stage-2 · Online Build (BMIET portal)
Shortlisted teams receive the exact brief and a tiny starter pack on the BMIET portal.
Window: 48 hours.
Deliverables (by track):
Web: HTML/CSS/JS page(s) with 2+ interactions; clean responsive layout; mobile + laptop friendly.
Python: small CLI helper with a simple menu and basic file read/write;
AI & Prompts: use Role / Constraints / Critique→Refine; keep a short before→after note; upload notebook/notes.
Submit a ZIP or repo link. Resubmits allowed till deadline; last one counts.
Stage-3 · Finale @ BMIET, Sonepat (offline)
Finalists present a 8-minute demo (5’ demo + 2’ Q&A + 1’ learning/switch).
Judging (100): Demo clarity 25 · Thoughtful choices/design 25 · Technical depth 25 · Impact & learning 25.
Eligibility & Teaming:
UG students (any branch, any year, recognized colleges/universities).
Solo or team of 2. Pick one track; you may fine-tune your choice in Stage-2 before upload.
Stage-2 access is via access code + college email sent only to shortlisted teams.
Rules & Fairness:
Complete the base task first; bonuses are optional.
You may use templates/starter code, but credit them in your README/notes.
AI help is allowed with clear disclosure of what/where/how it was used.
Plagiarism or uncredited reuse will lead to disqualification.
Tie-breakers: short-answer rubric → earliest valid submission → diversity of approach.
What we assess:
Does it work reliably (no “happy-path only”)?
Is the code/logic readable and organized?
Are design choices intentional (naming, structure, UX basics)?
Can you explain what changed from first attempt to final outcome?
Important dates (IST):
Stage-1 (Online on Unstop): 25 Sep 2025
Stage-2 (Online Build): 03 Oct → 05 Oct
Finale @ BMIET (offline): 31st Oct 2025
(Small adjustments may be communicated via email/Unstop message.)
Prizes & Certificates:
Prize from worth 25k pool: Winner, Runner-Up, Honourable Mention.
Certificates for all participants who complete the required submissions .

Name: Tamizh-A-Thon' 1.0
Organizer: SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST), Kattankulathur, Chennai
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Tamizh-A-Thon' 1.0
The
Tamil Computing Centre (TCC)
at SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST), in collaboration with the
International Forum for Information Technology in Tamil (INFITT)
, proudly presents
Tamizh-A-Thon 1.0
, a national-level hackathon under the theme:
“Innovating the Future of Tamil Computing.”
This initiative aims to foster innovation and creativity in Tamil computing, focusing on digital preservation, artificial intelligence, and advanced computing solutions. The hackathon encourages students and technology enthusiasts to develop tools and solutions that make the Tamil language more globally accessible while preserving its rich heritage. Participants will gain hands-on experience in solving real-world challenges, enhance technical and collaborative skills, and contribute to the digital transformation of Tamil language resources.
PRELIMINARY ROUND 1 REGISTRATION IS COMPLETELY FREE
REGISTRATION FEES:
₹500 only for second round
REGISTRATION FORM:
https://forms.gle/kCdSEus7JM3AXe6b8
Eligibility & Team Formation
Open to all college students across India, irrespective of academic discipline
Team size: 1–3 members
Inter-college and interdisciplinary teams are encouraged
Students with interests in AI/ML, programming, Tamil computing, OCR, NLP, and language digitization are highly encouraged
This ensures participants can exchange knowledge, leverage complementary skills, and create solutions that are both technically sound and culturally relevant.
Prizes & Benefits
Total Prize Pool: ₹1,00,000
1st Prize: ₹50,000
2nd Prize: ₹30,000
3rd Prize: ₹20,000
Additional Benefits:
Participation certificates for all teams
Mentorship from experts during the final round
Refreshments and logistical support for all finalists at SRMIST
Networking opportunities with faculty, industry experts, and peers in Tamil computing
Event Format
Preliminary Round (Online):
Teams submit a PPT describing their proposed solution to selected problem statements
Submissions reviewed by domain experts; feedback may be provided
Shortlisted teams qualify for the offline finals
Final Round (Offline @ SRMIST, Kattankulathur):
36-hour hackathon where teams develop working prototypes
Midway reviews to track progress and provide expert guidance
Final presentations and demonstrations evaluated for innovation, usability, and impact
Event Flow
13 September 2025: Preliminary Round (Online PPT Submission)
14 September 2025: Announcement of Prelims Results
19 September 2025: Inaugural Ceremony & Kick-off (Offline)
20 September 2025: Midway Reviews and Mentorship
21 September 2025: Final Demonstrations, Jury Evaluation & Prize Distribution
Problem Statements
Convert old font (16-bit) Tamil PDFs into 32-bit Unicode searchable files
Digitize Olai Suvadi manuscripts into modern Tamil letters
Decode classical Tamil poems for digital and educational use
Identify the age of Tamil lectures from font and writing style
Decode temple carvings and inscriptions using AI techniques
Present old Tamil poetry in voice format for accessibility
These problem statements are designed to challenge participants’ technical skills while promoting the digital preservation of Tamil heritage.
Rules & Guidelines
Teams must register before the deadline and pay ₹500 per team
Only one submission per team is allowed
Originality is mandatory; plagiarism leads to disqualification
Prelims PPT must clearly define the idea, methodology, and expected impact
Shortlisted teams must attend the offline finale at SRMIST
Final projects must be completed within the hackathon duration
Teams must attend midway reviews during finals
Jury decisions are final and binding
