Navachar - Code Fest 2025
Introduction:
On the occasion of Engineer’s Day (15th September 2025), the Entrepreneurship Cell of Meerut Institute of Technology is organizing Navachar - Code Fest 2025, a competitive coding fest hosted on Unstop.
The objective of this event is to provide students with a platform to showcase their problem-solving skills, enhance their logical thinking, and compete with the best coding talent in the college.
Whether you are a beginner or an advanced programmer, Navachar will help you learn, compete, and grow.
Event Details:
Event Name: Navachar – Code Fest 2025
Organized By: E-CELL MIT MEERUT
Date: 15th September 2025
Mode: Online (via Unstop Platform)
Eligibility: Open to all college students
Registration Fee: Free
Certificates:
Winner Certificate – Awarded to one winner from each year-based round
Participation Certificate – Provided to all participants
Event Format and Rounds:
Round 1 — MCQ Challenge
Date & Time: 15th September 2025
Duration: 30 minutes
Format: 30 Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs)
Topics Covered: Programming basics, logical reasoning, aptitude, and general problem-solving
Eligibility: Open to all participants
Shortlisting Criteria:
1st Year students will end their participation here
2nd, 3rd, and 4th Year students qualifying here will move to Round 2
Round 2 — Medium-Level Coding Challenge
Date & Time: 15th September 2025
Duration: 60 minutes
Format: Three coding problems
Topics Covered: Data structures, algorithms, and implementation-based coding
Eligibility: Only for 2nd, 3rd, and 4th Year students
Shortlisting Criteria:
2nd Year students will end their participation here
3rd and 4th Year students qualifying here will move to Round 3
Round 3 — Hard-Level Coding Challenge
Date & Time: 15th September 2025
Duration: 90 minutes
Format: Two advanced coding problems
Topics Covered: Optimized algorithms, dynamic programming, and problem-solving under constraints
Eligibility: Only for 3rd and 4th Year students
Eligibility Criteria:
Only college students are allowed to participate
Registration using the official college email ID is mandatory
Participants must select their correct year of study while registering
Participants are only allowed to attempt rounds applicable to their year
Rules and Guidelines:

Over an intensive 36-hour journey, innovators, developers, and creators will come together to transform bold ideas into impactful solutions. Armed with open-source tools, participants will explore and experiment with AI models, blockchain, VR/AR, gaming engines, IoT ecosystems, and cloud-native frameworks, building projects that redefine the boundaries of possibility.
VinHack offers diverse tracks—including Artificial Intelligence, Internet of Things, Gaming, Sustainability, and Futuristic Technology—providing teams with the freedom to innovate, disrupt, and inspire. From immersive virtual environments and autonomous systems to decentralized applications and sustainable technologies, this is your chance to dream big and build bigger.

This competition is designed to challenge and inspire budding data science enthusiasts. Every participant will receive a certificate of participation to acknowledge their efforts.

All attendees will get some cool, exclusive stickers to show off their love for data science.

A special recognition for the "Most Intuitive Learner."

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Ideaforge'25
Overview:
IDEAFORGE’25 is the flagship event of AEVA (AIML Empowered Visionary Alliance), designed to promote innovation, entrepreneurship, and startup culture.
This edition focuses on Startup Idea Pitching, where student teams present impactful solutions.
Winning teams will receive AEVA’s support and mentorship to transform their ideas into future startups.
Guidelines:
Eligibility:
Open to all AIML students.
Team Formation:
Each team must be cross-year.
Mandatory Composition: 1 First-year + 1 Second-year + 1 Third-year student.
Teams can have a maximum of 4 members (optional additional member from any year).
Mentorship:
Each team will be allocated a Final Year student as a Mentor.
Rounds:
Round 1: Abstract & PPT Submission + Preliminary Pitching
Teams must submit a PPT (5–7 slides) covering Problem, Solution, Innovation, Business/Impact Model, and Future Scope.
Duration: 5 mins presentation + 3 mins Q&A.
Top 10 teams will qualify for the final round.
Round 2: Grand Pitch Finale
Teams present their refined PPT in front of the jury.
Duration: 10 mins presentation + 5 mins Q&A.
Merits:
Rewards, E-Certificates, Internship Opportunities.
Startup Development Support:
Winning teams will receive incubation support, mentorship, and prototype assistance from AEVA.
Rules:
Team composition rules (cross-year) must be strictly followed.
Abstract & PPT submission is mandatory for participation.
Pitches must be original and innovative; plagiarism leads to disqualification.
PPTs must follow the given structure: Problem → Solution → Innovation → Feasibility → Future Scope.
Time limits will be strictly monitored.
Mentors may guide but not directly contribute to the content.
Jury’s decision will be final.

Objective:
To challenge participants in fields of cybersecurity including cryptography, reverse engineering, web exploitation, forensics, and binary exploitation in a safe and ethical environment.
Formats:
Jeopardy Style – Multiple categories with increasing difficulty.
Attack-Defense – Teams defend their systems while attacking others.

Time-based – Challenges solved in fixed duration (e.g., 3 hours )
Event Setup & Challenge Deployment
Infrastructure:

creativity meets technology
. It’s the perfect platform for students, developers, and tech enthusiasts to come together, brainstorm innovative ideas, and build real-world solutions.

. With multiple rounds focusing on

capable of solving real-world problems under stringent constraints.

Teams must design a UAV that can:
Take off
from a designated launch zone.
Navigate autonomously or semi-autonomously
to a targeted payload drop zone (10m x 10m).
Accurately drop payloads (water)
mid-flight.
Minimize mission time
while ensuring safety and structural integrity of the airframe.

and
disaster relief operations
, making it a true test of
engineering design, precision control, and mission execution
.

disqualification
. Professional conduct is mandatory at all times.

.

Quality of design reports, technical presentations, and collaboration.

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Tech Geeks-Found
Tech Geeks Found is proud to announce an exclusive online competition designed to foster innovation, teamwork, and problem-solving among students and young professionals. This event provides a national platform for participants to showcase their talent, enhance their technical skills, and gain recognition for their creativity.
This opportunity is more than just a competition — it’s a journey of learning, experimenting, and collaborating with like-minded peers. Participants will explore real-world challenges, develop innovative solutions, and be recognized for their efforts. Whether you are passionate about coding, design, or creative problem-solving, this is your chance to stand out.
Rules & Guidelines
The competition will be conducted entirely online.
The hackathon will be hosted on Unstop.
Teams must consist of 2–5 members. Solo participation is not permitted.
All participants must be currently enrolled students (school, college, or university) or recent graduates.

Competition Structure
The competition consists of four key rounds designed to assess creativity, technical skills, and collaboration:
Round 1: Problem Statement Release:
The official problem statement will be shared with all registered teams. Teams must analyze the challenge and design their approach.
Round 2: Project Submission:
Teams will collaboratively develop their projects and submit them online before the deadline. Submissions must strictly follow the format and requirements provided by the organizers.
Round 3: Results Announcement:
The judging panel will evaluate the submissions based on creativity, innovation, technical feasibility, and execution. The top shortlisted teams will be announced online.
Round 4: Winner Announcement & Rewards:
Final winners will be revealed, followed by online recognition and distribution of rewards. Exclusive merchandise, goodies, and certificates will be awarded to the top-performing teams.
Registration Details
Registration is free of cost and open to students across the country.
Teams must register online by completing the official registration form.
Once registered, teams will receive a confirmation email with further instructions.

Name: Web Hackathon Challenge
Organizer: Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Bhubaneswar
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Web Hackathon Challenge
General Rules:
Participants can register individually or in teams (maximum of
4
members per team).
The hackathon theme and problem statements will be announced at the start of the event. All development must take place within the 24-hour duration—pre-existing projects are not allowed.
Use of third-party libraries, APIs, and frameworks is allowed but must be disclosed.
AI-generated code/assets are permitted only if disclosed and properly credited.
Team Formation:
Each team must designate a team leader for communication with organizers.
Participants cannot be part of multiple teams.
Teams cannot merge or split after registration.
Development & Coding Guidelines:
Original work only—projects must be built from scratch during the hackathon. Pre-existing boilerplate code (like UI templates) can be used, but must be disclosed.
Plagiarism is strictly prohibited—any detected plagiarism will result in immediate disqualification.
Offensive, illegal, or unethical content is strictly not allowed.
Submissions must be functional—non-working prototypes will not be considered.
Submission Requirements:
Teams must submit their projects before the deadline.
Submission must include:
Working demo (hosted link or locally runnable build)
GitHub repository (with clear documentation)
Presentation slides (max 5 slides) explaining the idea, tech stack, and implementation
Team details and roles
Late submissions will not be accepted.
Judging Criteria:
Projects will be evaluated based on:
Innovation & Creativity (20%) – Uniqueness of the idea
Functionality & Execution (30%) – Working demo, responsiveness, and usability
Technical Implementation (25%) – Code quality, scalability, and best practices
UI/UX Design (15%) – User-friendly interface and visual appeal
Adherence to Theme (10%) – Relevance to the given challenge
Code of Conduct:
Respect all participants, mentors, and organizers.
Collaboration is encouraged, but no external help or outsourcing is allowed.
Hacking, cheating, or any form of unethical behaviour will result in disqualification.
If an issue arises, teams should report to the organizers immediately.
Hardware & Internet Usage:
Participants must use their own laptops/devices—external servers can be used if disclosed.
No DDoS attacks, unauthorized API abuse, or network manipulation allowed.
Organizers are not responsible for hardware failures or lost progress—backup your work frequently.
Intellectual Property & Rights:
Participants own their projects, but organizers reserve the right to showcase the work for promotional purposes.

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Design Marathon
General Rules & Guidelines
Participants can register individually or in teams (maximum of
4
members per team).
The design theme and problem statements will be announced at the start of the event.
Use of third-party design tools, assets, and libraries (e.g., stock images, fonts, UI kits) is allowed but must be disclosed.
AI-generated designs/assets are permitted only if disclosed and properly credited.
Team Formation:
Each team must designate a team leader for communication with organizers.
Participants cannot be part of multiple teams.
Teams cannot merge or split after registration.
Design & Development Guidelines:
Original work only—projects must be created from scratch during the marathon. Pre-existing design templates may be referenced but not directly reused—any such references must be disclosed.
Plagiarism is strictly prohibited—any detected plagiarism will result in immediate disqualification.
Offensive, illegal, or unethical content is strictly not allowed.
Submissions must be complete and showcase a coherent branding identity.
Submission Requirements:
Final design files (Figma/Adobe XD/Illustrator/Photoshop or high-quality image exports).
Presentation slides (max 5 slides) explaining:
Idea and inspiration
Design process & tools used
Brand identity elements and rationale
Team details and roles
Projects will be evaluated based on:
Creativity & Originality (20%) – Distinctiveness of the branding approach
Brand Identity & Consistency (30%) – Cohesion across all elements (logo, visuals, messaging)
Visual Appeal & Aesthetics (20%) – Design quality, color use, typography, and overall look
Communication & Clarity (15%) – How effectively the branding conveys the intended message
Adherence to Theme (15%) – Relevance to the given challenge
Code of Conduct:
Respect all participants, mentors, and organizers.
Collaboration is encouraged, but no external help or outsourcing is allowed.
Any form of cheating, misrepresentation, or unethical behaviour will result in disqualification.
If an issue arises, teams should report to the organizers immediately.
Tools & Resources Usage:
Participants must use their own laptops/devices—cloud-based platforms are allowed if disclosed.
Use of copyrighted materials without proper license is strictly prohibited.
Organizers are not responsible for hardware/software failures or lost progress—backup your work frequently.

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Callidus - Game Jam
Event Overview
Callidus – Game Jam (Infotsav Special Edition) is an online competition where participants create a game and submit within the 7 day time period.
Theme: The Odds Are Against You
Open to beginners and experienced developers alike.
All submitted games will be showcased live during Infotsav 2025, with audience testing, reviews, and results.
Registration Process
Register on Unstop – mandatory for official ranking and prizes.
Register on Itch.io on our jam page(https://itch.io/jam/callidus-jam)
Provide your Itch username before registration closes and share the game name when decided.
Submissions must be uploaded to Itch.io.
Teams registering only on Itch.io without Unstop will not be considered for prizes.
Event Flow
3rd October: Registrations confirmed (Unstop + Itch.io) and submission of Itch username.
5th October: Idea and progress review (feedback round, not elimination).
9th October: Final submission deadline (upload on Itch.io + share link on Unstop).
10th–12th October: Judging and audience testing during Infotsav.
15th October: Results declaration.
Team & Duration
Team size: 1–3 participants.
Duration: 7 days to submit the game.
Submission platform: Itch.io (https://itch.io/jam/callidus-jam).
Rules & Guidelines
Participants may use any game engine, tool, or framework.
Assets must be either custom-made or open-source with non-restrictive licenses (credit creators where possible).
Winning teams must share project files (GitHub, Drive, or zipped copy) for verification.
Documentation of resources used must be maintained.
Games must align with the announced theme or interpretation of “The Odds Are Against You.”
Plagiarism or use of unauthorized copyrighted assets will lead to disqualification.
You may start working on your idea/game before the event begins.
Judging Criteria
Theme alignment – how well the game reflects the theme.
Storyline – creativity and immersion in narrative and world-building.
Game mechanics and flow – engagement, smoothness, and playability.
Creativity – innovation in design, concept, and execution.
Rewards & Recognition
First place: ₹3000 (cash prize).
Second place: ₹2000 (cash prize).
All games will be showcased live during Infotsav.
Eligibility
Open to all students and game enthusiasts with basic coding or art skills.
No prior professional experience required.
Get ready to code, create, and compete in Callidus – Game Jam (Infotsav Special Edition) with the theme: The Odds Are Against You.

Event Overview
CodeDuelz is a Head-to-Head Competitive Programming Battle where teams battle through multiple knockout rounds, crowning a DSA winner team in Central Asia's largest technical fest TantraFeista’25, at IIIT Nagpur. The event combines the thrill of DSA problem-solving with the intensity of direct face to face duels with top coding teams from all over the nation. Platform: Unstop
Format: MCQs + short coding/problem-solving.
Duration: 30–45 minutes.
Selection: Top 16 teams qualify for Round 2.
Round 2 : Offline Knockout Qualifier
Venue: Computer Labs, IIIT Nagpur
Teams: 16
Format: Head-to-head coding duels on CodeDuelz.
Advancement: 8 winning teams qualify; 8 eliminated.

Venue: Computer Labs, IIIT Nagpur
Teams: 8

Advancement: 4 winning teams qualify; 4 eliminated.

Venue: Main Stage

2 winners → Finals (Round 5 – 1st place match).
2 losers → 3rd Place Match (Round 5).

Venue: Main Stage
Finals: 2 winning teams from Round 4 compete for 1st place.
3rd Place Match: 2 losing teams from Round 4 compete for 3rd place.
Judging and Scoring:
Round 1 (Quiz): Auto-scored on Unstop.
Rounds 2–5 (Coding Duels):
Judgement will be done on the CodeDuelz platform.
The score and results displayed on the platform will be considered final.

Are you ready to turn bold ideas into real-world ventures?
When Technology Becomes a Venture is not just another competition. It’s a launchpad for innovation, creativity, and entrepreneurship.
In today’s world, technology is the heartbeat of every successful business. From AI-powered healthcare solutions to IoT-enabled smart cities, from Blockchain-driven security to data-powered retail giants every great venture begins with an idea that blends cutting-edge tech with a profitable business model.

Name: Quadruped Challenge
Organizer: Indian Institute of Technology, Bhubaneswar
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Quadruped Challenge
About the Challenge
This hackathon challenges participants to develop a
ROS2-based control and navigation stack
for the Spot robot in a simulated environment. The focus is on
autonomous navigation, obstacle avoidance, and robust software development using ROS2
. The event is fully simulation-based.
Team formation:
A team can have one to four persons. The members of the team can be from  different colleges.
Problem Statement
Develop a ROS2-based software stack for the Spot robot that enables:
Autonomous navigation
in a given simulated environment.
Basic path planning
and obstacle avoidance.
Integration
with the provided environment using ROS2-compatible simulation software.
The environment will be provided by the organizing team on
Day 1
of the hackathon.
Environment
A single simulation environment will be provided by the organizers on
Day 1
.
Teams must integrate their software solution with this environment and demonstrate Spot’s ability to
navigate autonomously
.
Hackathon Timeline
2 Weeks Before Hackathon
: Release of the Problem Statement Booklet and Spot GitHub repository link. Teams can start planning, exploring the ROS2 stack, and setting up their development environment.
Day 1 (On Campus)
: Release of the simulation environment, including the designated
start point
and
end point
. Teams begin development and integration.
Day 2 (On Campus)
: Participants must submit:
A
GitHub repository
containing the complete ROS2 package with clear setup and usage instructions.
A
demonstration video
showing Spot navigating from the start point to the end point.
A
concise presentation
explaining the technical approach, algorithms, and key implementation decisions.
Specifications
Simulation Platform:
ROS2-compatible simulator (Gazebo)
Robot Type:
Spot robot (quadruped) in simulation
Deliverables
ROS2 Package
Upload a complete package to a
public GitHub repository
.
Include a detailed
README
with installation, setup, and execution instructions.
Demonstration Video
Show the robot navigating the provided environment from the start point to the end point.
Technical Presentation (PowerPoint)
Delivered to the judging panel on
Day 2
.
Must cover:
Overall software architecture
AI/ML algorithms (if any) used for navigation and obstacle avoidance
Key implementation details and design choices
Evaluation Criteria
Total Points: 100
Criteria
Description
Weightage
Effectiveness
Obstacle avoidance accuracy, autonomous task completion, real-time responsiveness
40%
Scalability
Environment adaptability, efficient resource management
30%
Innovation & Technical Depth
Algorithm complexity, problem-specific optimizations
30%
Submission Guidelines
All code must be hosted on a
public GitHub repository
.
The
README
must include installation, setup, and usage instructions.
Submit a
demonstration video
showing Spot navigating the environment.
Prepare a
final presentation
to be delivered to the judging panel on
Day 2
.

Everything you need to know about
Elevate
For external participants, please register through the official event page:

ELEVATE is a national-level student design competition where innovators from across India will design and present levitation systems for Hyperloop and Maglev applications.
Detailed Description
About the Competition
Hosted by
Team Levitate Hyperloop
, the winners of Asia's first Hyperloop competition (GHC 2025),
ELEVATE
is a two-day national-level student design competition and technical symposium. As a flagship event of
graVITas’25
at VIT Vellore, ELEVATE is your opportunity to push the boundaries of future transportation.
This event challenges undergraduate and postgraduate students from all over India to conceptualize and develop innovative levitation systems. Participants will present their CAD/CAE-based designs and engage directly with top industry professionals and academic experts. This is more than just a competition; it's a chance to learn, network, and showcase your engineering prowess.
What You'll Experience:
Design Challenge:
Apply your knowledge to design next-generation levitation systems for Hyperloop and Maglev technologies.
Expert Talks:
Gain invaluable insights from leading figures in the transport and energy sectors.
Interactive Sessions:
Engage in a dynamic environment where you can ask questions and discuss ideas with experts.
Networking:
Connect with like-minded peers and professionals who are shaping the future of engineering.
Eligibility Criteria
Open to
all undergraduate and postgraduate students
from across India.
Rules & Registration
Registration:
All participants must register individually for the event on the graVITas website.
Team Size:
Teams must consist of a
maximum of 4 members
.
Team Finalization:
Although registration is individual, a Google Form will be circulated later to collect final team details and map members together.
Eligibility:
All team members must register for the Day 1 event on
26th September 2025
to be eligible for the competition.
Skills to be Assessed
Your team will be evaluated on the following key areas:
Engineering Design Thinking:
Your ability to conceptualize and develop innovative levitation systems.
CAD & CAE Proficiency:
Your competence in using simulation tools to validate and optimize your designs.
Systems Integration:
Your understanding of how levitation, propulsion, and structural elements work together in a cohesive system.
Sustainability & Feasibility Analysis:
Your evaluation of energy efficiency, material usage, and the real-world viability of your design.
Presentation & Communication:
Your clarity in conveying technical ideas and your ability to defend them during questioning.

CICADA
Team Formation:
Teams can consist of
2 to 4 members
.
Each team must designate a
Team Leader
to serve as the primary contact.
Team composition must remain the same throughout the entire event. No replacements are allowed.
Themes:
Gen AI
Machine Learning
Deep Learning
Computer Vision
Internet of Things (IoT)
Smart Automation
Cybersecurity
Hackathon Format & Process:
The hackathon is a continuous
24-hour coding event
.
Problem statements will be provided at the beginning of the event.
Participants will be evaluated based on creativity, technical execution, feasibility, and presentation skills.
The total number of evaluation rounds will be announced during the inauguration ceremony.
Project Development Guidelines:
Original Work:
All code must be developed during the 24-hour hackathon period. Submitting pre-developed projects will lead to immediate disqualification.
Third-Party Resources:
Teams are permitted to use publicly available libraries, APIs, and frameworks.
AI-Generated Code:
Relying completely on AI-generated code is not encouraged.
Code Ownership:
All code developed must be the original work of the team. Participants cannot use code or assets created by others without proper permissions or licenses.
Code Submission:
Only one repository is allowed per team.
Teams must push commits
every 2 hours
.
Pushing or pulling from the repository is prohibited outside the designated coding period.
The final GitHub repository link must be submitted for evaluation.
General Guidelines:
All participants must bring their own laptops, chargers, and any other necessary accessories.
Participants must carry their
college ID card
for verification at all times.
Event ID cards and the provided T-shirts must be worn throughout the event.
Seeking help from mentors or other teams is
not allowed
. Cross-team collaboration is prohibited.
Roaming around the college campus during the event is not permitted.
Misconduct or any damage to property will lead to penalties and potential disqualification.
The use of unwanted substances (including drugs or alcohol) is strictly prohibited and will result in immediate disqualification.
Any form of cheating or plagiarism will lead to immediate disqualification.
The decision of the jury is final.
Judging Criteria:
Innovation and Uniqueness
Technical Implementation
User Experience
Importance of the Problem Solved
Practicality and Feasibility
Presentation Skills
Quality of the Product
Scalability
Presentation:
Each team must present their project to the judging panel.
The presentation should demonstrate the project's functionality, features, and innovative aspects.
Teams should explain the technical challenges they faced and how they were overcome.
Simple ideas or minor modifications of existing solutions will not be accepted.

Name: Game Dev Hackathon (DevClub IITD X Krafton)
Organizer: Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Delhi
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Game Dev Hackathon (DevClub IITD X Krafton)
Welcome to the DevClub x Krafton Game Jam!
Hosted by DevClub, IIT Delhi, this Game Jam is the ultimate playground where creativity meets game development. Over an intense sprint, you and your team will design and build a game that doesn’t just look good—but breathes life into its mechanics, narrative, and design through the themes of the Jam.
This isn’t just about coding—it’s about weaving stories, bending rules, and crafting experiences that players will remember. Whether you’re experimenting with Unity, Unreal, Godot, or even your own custom engine, this is your chance to push boundaries, collaborate, and bring bold ideas to life.
Rewards & Benefits:
Massive Prize Pool: Compete for a share of the ₹1L prize pool and exclusive merchandise.
Showcase Your Creativity: Stand out in front of peers and industry leaders, and add a polished game to your portfolio.
Compete with the Best: Team up or go solo, and test your ideas against some of the brightest minds at IIT Delhi and beyond.
Are you ready to turn imagination into gameplay, and gameplay into an unforgettable story?
Let the Jam begin.
Themes
Participants are required to
design and develop a game
that meaningfully incorporates
at least two of the following themes:
Thread of Time
Rewind
Changing Rules
Fragile
Symmetry
Eligibility:
Teams can consist of 1 to 4 members.
All participants must register on the Unstop platform before the registration deadline.
Timeline:
Preliminary Round 1 (Online):
Registration: 18th September - 23th September 2025
Problem Statement Release: 18th September 2025
Submission Window: 18th - 25th September 2025
Final Round (On-Campus  at IIT Delhi):
Date: 29th September 2025
Duration: 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM
Prizes:
Total Prize Pool of INR 1,00,000
First Place: INR 50,000 cash prize.
Second Place: INR 30,000 cash prize.
Third Place: INR 20,000 cash prize.
Selected students may be eligible for internship opportunities with Krafton.
Important Note:
The chosen themes should not simply act as
visual elements
or
background decorations
. Instead, they must play an
integral role in the core gameplay, mechanics, or narrative design
.
Teams are free to
interpret and apply the themes creatively
.
The
originality of implementation
will be a key factor in the evaluation process.
The
problem statement
for the prelims has been released on Unstop. You can find it in the Attachments sections by scrolling down. Please also join the Whatsapp group via the link in problem statement to stay updated and get your doubts clarified.
Submission Guidelines
The final
submission zip/release
must include:
Playable Build
(exe/apk/html5) OR full demo video.
README.md
file with:
Controls.
System requirements.
Installation/launch steps.
Known issues (if any).
Credits.txt
→ Asset attributions and licenses.
Presentation slides or video link.
Metadata file
→ Team information and game description.
(Optional)
Source code link or GitHub repository (mention license clearly).
Technical Guidelines for Builds
Windows
: Zip the complete build folder (Unity builds must include the
_Data
folder). Add a
run.bat
or clear launch steps in the README.
Android
: Mention minimum SDK target and add installation instructions for devices (including enabling "install from unknown sources").
Web/HTML5
: Test locally (
index.html
) and on itch.io. Provide fallback
.zip
in case hosting fails.
File Size Limit
: Builds exceeding
1 GB
require prior approval, or a compact demo version must be submitted along with the gameplay video.
Judging Criteria
Submissions will be evaluated on the following parameters:
Originality & Creativity
– How unique and innovative is the concept?
Gameplay & Fun Factor
– Is it engaging, enjoyable, and replayable?
Theme Integration
– How clearly and effectively are the chosen themes implemented?
Completeness & Polish
– Quality of art, sound, UI, overall polish, and bug-free execution.
Submission Rules and Timeline
Submission Deadline
: All entries must be submitted via the official submission portal
on or before 25th September
.
Late submissions will not be accepted.
Submission Requirements
:
Provide a link to the build (itch.io/GitHub) OR upload the complete submission zip to the portal.
On-Campus Showcase
: Shortlisted teams will be invited to present and demo their projects
on 29th September
.
This hackathon is designed not just as a competition but as an opportunity to
explore creativity, strengthen teamwork, and push the boundaries of your imagination
. We encourage every participant to take this challenge as a chance to learn new skills, experiment with bold ideas, and create something truly memorable.
No matter the outcome, completing this journey will give you valuable experience in game development, collaboration, and problem-solving. We wish you
the very best of luck
and hope this becomes a
rewarding and unforgettable experience
for you and your team.

Name: Rewind and Recode (Gujarat)
Organizer: International Institute of Information Technology, Bhubaneswar
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Rewind and Recode (Gujarat)
Join If your College Comes Under Gujarat
Step into the world of innovation, creativity, and non-stop coding at the
Rewind and Recode Hackathon
, a premier
National Level Hackathon
hosted by the esteemed
International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Bhubaneswar
. This one-of-a-kind event brings together the best and brightest problem solvers, coders, and innovators from across India to tackle real-world challenges with disruptive technology and bold ideas.
Scheduled from 7th to 9th November 2025, Rewind and Recode isn’t just another hackathon — it’s a launchpad for student innovation, a battlefield of logic and creativity, and a celebration of India’s next-generation tech talent. With 24 hours of non-stop ideation, coding, collaboration, and execution, participants will compete head-to-head to build impactful, scalable, and solution-oriented tech projects.
Whether you're from a premier institute like IIT, NIT, IIIT or a passionate developer from any engineering college across India, if you're driven to create meaningful solutions, this is your stage. This hackathon invites teams from every corner of the country, ensuring a diverse pool of minds working across multiple themes — including healthcare, sustainability, agriculture, finance, education, cybersecurity, and more.
The problem statements will be released on 7th November at 8:00 AM IST, marking the beginning of the hackathon. Teams will then engage in a high-intensity sprint — analyzing, brainstorming, coding, and pitching — all within a tightly packed 24-hour window. The atmosphere will be buzzing with energy at the on-site venue of IIIT Bhubaneswar, as students code side-by-side with peers, under the mentorship of experienced tech experts and industry leaders.
But it’s not just about writing code. Rewind and Recode emphasizes
end-to-end problem solving

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Vyto HackClash 3.0
Vyto HackClash 3.0: The Ultimate 36-Hour Hackathon
(Under VytoBlitz 2.0 – National Level Tech Fest)
Vyto HackClash 3.0 is an exciting 36-hour hackathon organized by VytoFlow Tech under the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, I.T.S Engineering College, as part of VytoBlitz 2.0 – National Level Tech Fest.
It is designed to inspire innovation, creativity, and collaboration among some of the brightest technical minds. Participants will solve real-world challenges, showcasing their talent in emerging technologies and product development.
Previous Event Glimpses & Reach
Our previous editions have set a benchmark of excellence and participation:
Edition
Registrations
Impressions
Mode
Highlight

744 teams
1,07,458
Offline
One of the largest regional hackathons with vibrant on-campus buzz.
Vyto HackClash 2.0
470 teams
43,686
Online
Demonstrated strong national-level engagement even in a virtual format.
Vyto HackClash has built a remarkable legacy through its earlier editions, consistently attracting passionate innovators from across the country. Vyto HackClash 1.0 achieved an outstanding 744 team registrations with team sizes of 2–4 members, generating an incredible 1,07,458 impressions, making it one of the most talked-about offline hackathons in the region. Vyto HackClash 2.0, conducted entirely online, continued the momentum with 470 registered teams and 43,686 impressions, proving its capability to engage talent nationwide even in virtual settings. These achievements highlight the growing trust, visibility, and impact of the VytoFlow Tech Community and set the stage for Vyto HackClash 3.0 to be even bigger and more inspiring under VytoBlitz 2.0 – National Level Tech Fest.

Inspire young innovators to create impactful technical solutions for real-life problems.

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Hovercraft Challenge
Space exploration often requires a probe to be operated remotely over a surface. The probe, which in most cases is referred to as a rover, passes through hurdles that mere humans cannot withstand. One can’t help but be fascinated by the amount of work that needs to be put in to make rovers that are that endurant and versatile. The Hovercraft Challenge is designed as an interdisciplinary technical competition, participants will design and construct small-scale hoverpods capable of navigating a custom-built obstacle track.

Name: eSim Marathon - Circuit Design & Simulation with IHP SG13G2
Organizer: Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Bombay
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
eSim Marathon - Circuit Design & Simulation with IHP SG13G2
Register Now!
Join the
eSim & IHP Marathon
– Open to participants from India and abroad.
Tap to Register via Google Form:
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdm5Me47DZd8qZv7URnN9i92bDpyr8WBVyG0lzj-eL5H9dLnw/viewform
Participants must first fill out the Google Form (copy and paste the link in your browser) before starting.
Unstop registration can also be done, but Google Form registration is
mandatory
to get access to the exclusive webinar.
Introduction:
The design and verification of integrated circuits (ICs) have traditionally relied on costly proprietary tools and closed foundry ecosystems.
This has limited access for students, researchers, and startups—especially in developing regions—who wish to explore VLSI design.
To address this gap, FOSSEE, IIT Bombay developed
eSim
, an open-source EDA tool supporting schematic design, simulation, and PCB development.
In parallel,
IHP Microelectronics, Germany
released the
SG13G2 PDK
under an open-source license, enabling high-frequency analog, RF, and digital circuit design.
About the Marathon:
The
eSim Marathon – Circuit Design & Simulation with IHP SG13G2
is a nationwide circuit design competition organized by
FOSSEE, IIT Bombay
.
Participants will use
eSim
with the
IHP SG13G2 PDK
to design and simulate analog, digital, and mixed-signal circuits.
The event bridges theoretical learning with practical chip design experience through an open-source flow.
Objectives & Learning Outcomes:
Gain hands-on experience with a real-world foundry PDK.
Design and simulate analog, digital, and mixed-signal circuits using open-source tools.
Develop skills aligned with industry and academic requirements.
Contribute to the global open-source silicon movement.
Build capacity in VLSI using open infrastructure aligned with national and global educational goals.
Key Highlights:
Tool Used:
eSim, an open-source alternative to commercial tools like OrCAD.
PDK Used:
IHP SG13G2, enabling design of analog, digital, and RF circuits at the 130 nm node.
Objective:
Design, simulate, and submit functional circuits.
Eligibility:
Open to students, hobbyists, and early-career professionals.
Outcomes:
Practical exposure to VLSI workflows, schematic design, simulations, and documentation.
Why eSim?
Free & Open Source: No license restrictions; ideal for students, researchers, and professionals.
Comprehensive Learning Resources: Includes Spoken Tutorial training for quick upskilling.
Commercial Use Ready: Applicable for consulting and company projects without licensing concerns.
Entrepreneur Friendly: Affordable option for startups and SMEs.
Flexible Migration: Easy switch to commercial software when required.
Why IHP?
Leading Research Institute specializing in advanced BiCMOS semiconductor technologies.
Historic Open Source Release: First-ever industrial-grade PDK (SG13G2) released as open source for academia, enthusiasts, and researchers worldwide.

Brief Description
Showcase your groundbreaking cybersecurity idea at Cyber Kavach 2025!

About the Competition
As a flagship event of our annual security conference, the Cyber Kavach 2025 ideaExpo is where innovation meets opportunity.
The OWASP PCCOE Student Chapter invites you to present your forward-thinking solutions to today's most pressing digital threats.
This is your platform to showcase a brilliant idea, a research concept, or an early-stage prototype.
We are looking for innovators who can think critically and creatively to solve real-world security challenges.

Name: CodeXcape
Organizer: Vellore Institute of Technology (VIT), Vellore
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
CodeXcape
For external participants, please register through the official event page:
https://gravitas.vit.ac.in/events/5ff093ef-fd7b-444f-b4e6-7decfe6db38f
A high-energy, 24-hour adventure where teams navigate three thrilling rounds—Jeopardy, Scotland Yard, and a Hackathon—to test their knowledge, strategy, and problem-solving skills.
Detailed Description:
Welcome to CodeXcape, a unique 24-hour challenge that blends trivia, strategy, and innovation into one epic event. This isn't just a hackathon; it's an adventure where every decision, from the first trivia question to the final line of code, matters.
Your journey unfolds across three thrilling rounds:
Round 1: Jeopardy The adventure begins with a classic! Teams will compete in a fast-paced trivia round, answering questions of varying difficulty. Every correct answer earns MozCoins—our event's currency—while a wrong one can lead to losses. This round is a test of your knowledge and quick thinking.
Round 2: Scotland Yard Next, the game shifts to strategy. Five detective teams will enter the world of Scotland Yard, where they must track down the elusive Mr. X. MozCoins become your most valuable asset here, allowing you to reveal locations, gain strategic advantages, or evade capture in a high-stakes battle of wits and resource management.
Round 3: The Hackathon Finale The final challenge is a high-stakes hackathon. Teams will receive randomized problem statements and must use their creativity and problem-solving skills to build a solution. Here, your accumulated MozCoins are a game-changer! Spend them to swap your problem statement, buy exclusive mentorship from experts, or unlock extra development time.
Winning Criteria:
The ultimate winners are decided by a composite score:
80% weight on your Hackathon project's performance.
20% weight on your remaining MozCoins.
Every move you make has a lasting impact. Are you ready to escape the ordinary and prove your mettle?
Rules & Regulations:
AI Tool Usage: Use of AI tools is strictly prohibited during the Jeopardy round. While not encouraged during the hackathon, their use is permitted. However, the dispatch of mentors from the club is at the discretion of the organizing team.
Laptop Policy: Only one laptop per team is allowed during the Jeopardy and Scotland Yard rounds. The team must work as a single unit.
Eligibility & Team Size:
Eligibility: The event is open to anyone.
Team Size: Teams must consist of 3-5 members.

Get recognized with rankings, certificates, and exclusive rewards.
Marking Scheme:
The assessment consists of 6 coding questions:
2 Hard Questions – 25 marks each
2 Intermediate Questions – 15 marks each
2 Easy Questions – 10 marks each
Total Marks: 100
Time Duration - 2 hrs
No negative marking
Marks are awarded only if your solution passes all test cases and is accepted by the system
No marks will be given for partially correct solutions or submissions that only pass the sample test cases
Competition Instructions:
The assessment will open in full-screen mode. Tab-switching or background activity is strictly prohibited and may lead to disqualification.
Once you begin the test, you must complete it in one sitting. No breaks or retakes will be allowed.
Only the first submission for each question will be considered. You won’t be able to edit or resubmit your answers.
Use of AI tools, copied code from the internet, or external assistance is strictly forbidden. Any form of plagiarism will result in immediate disqualification.
Ensure a stable internet connection and avoid refreshing the page or closing the browser tab during the assessment.

HACKSETU 1.0

Teams must submit a PPT presentation of their innovative idea at the time of registration.
The PPT should clearly include:
Problem statement, proposed solution, technology stack, and potential impact.
Note:
This round is for idea evaluation only. No coding or prototype development is expected.
Shortlisted teams will be notified via email for the offline grand finale.
Round 2: Grand Finale (Offline Hackathon at Amity University, Gwalior)
Shortlisted teams will participate in a 24-hour coding marathon on campus.
Teams will develop a working prototype of their submitted idea.
Mentor checkpoints will be conducted throughout the event to guide teams.
Final presentations and demos will decide the winners.
Guidelines – HACKSETU 1.0:
Team Size:
Up to 4 members.
Eligibility:
Open to students, developers, and innovators from across India.
Themes:

Judging Criteria:
Innovation, feasibility, impact, user experience, clarity of presentation, and quality of prototype.

Name: Rewind and Recode (Jharkhand)
Organizer: International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Bhubaneswar
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Rewind and Recode (Jharkhand)
Register only if your college is in Jharkhand
Step into the world of innovation, creativity, and non-stop coding at the Rewind and Recode Hackathon, a premier National Level Hackathon hosted by the esteemed International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Bhubaneswar. This one-of-a-kind event brings together the best and brightest problem solvers, coders, and innovators from across India to tackle real-world challenges with disruptive technology and bold ideas.
Scheduled from 7th to 9th November 2025, Rewind and Recode isn’t just another hackathon — it’s a launchpad for student innovation, a battlefield of logic and creativity, and a celebration of India’s next-generation tech talent. With 24 hours of non-stop ideation, coding, collaboration, and execution, participants will compete head-to-head to build impactful, scalable, and solution-oriented tech projects.
Whether you're from a premier institute like IIT, NIT, IIIT or a passionate developer from any engineering college across India, if you're driven to create meaningful solutions, this is your stage. This hackathon invites teams from every corner of the country, ensuring a diverse pool of minds working across multiple themes — including healthcare, sustainability, agriculture, finance, education, cybersecurity, and more.
The problem statements will be released on 7th November at 8:00 AM IST, marking the beginning of the hackathon. Teams will then engage in a high-intensity sprint — analyzing, brainstorming, coding, and pitching — all within a tightly packed 24-hour window. The atmosphere will be buzzing with energy at the on-site venue of IIIT Bhubaneswar, as students code side-by-side with peers, under the mentorship of experienced tech experts and industry leaders.
But it’s not just about writing code. Rewind and Recode emphasizes end-to-end problem solving — identifying core pain points, proposing real-world solutions, justifying tech stacks, building intuitive UIs, and pitching like professionals. It’s a full-cycle product development experience compressed into 24 hours — intense, insightful, and insanely rewarding.
Registrations open in August 2025, and interested teams are advised to register early through the official portal. Once registered, teams will be eligible for further updates, problem statements, and participation in internal shortlisting (if applicable via respective institutions). Ensure your team is formed, registered, and ready before the hackathon begins!
What’s in it for you?
Besides exciting prizes, certificates, and recognition, you get a chance to:
Network with students and tech leaders from across India
Receive mentorship from professionals
Test your skills in a real-world, time-constrained setup
Strengthen your portfolio and technical credibility
This is not just a hackathon — it’s your chance to shine on the national stage.
So, gather your team. Register this August 2025.
And from 7th to 9th November, come to IIIT Bhubaneswar to Rewind what you know… and Recode what the world needs.
Let’s innovate. Let’s hustle. Let’s Hack.

Name: Startup Hackathon 2.0 - Self Employing youth for a better Future.
Organizer: Mitraroop Foundation
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Startup Hackathon 2.0 - Self Employing youth for a better Future.
Do you harbor the passion for entrepreneurship but struggle with ideation paralysis or the minimum viable product (MVP) gap? Stop waiting for the perfect moment. This isn't just a competition; it's your definitive on-ramp into the high-octane world of venture creation. This event eliminates the initial friction by providing the opportunity set—you supply the execution prowess.
Event & Eligibility: Your Gateway to Disruption
This immersive, high-velocity Startup Hackathon is your chance to pivot from aspiring enthusiast to founder-in-motion.
Date of Deployment: Sunday, August 9th, starting at 9:00 AM sharp.
Team Configuration: Form your A-team with 1–4 members (a minimum of one dedicated coder/developer is mandatory for technical viability).
Participant Spectrum: Open to all innovators, from university students seeking experiential learning to working professionals aiming for a career pivot. Cross-functional and cross-institutional teams are highly encouraged.
The Competition Structure: Three Phases of Execution (Online):
The entire hackathon will be conducted online and structured into three intense, time-boxed phases designed to mimic real-world startup sprints:
Phase 1: Idea Selection (1 Hour): Every team will be granted exclusive access to a curated portfolio of 25+ pre-vetted startup ideas. Your objective is to perform rapid concept validation and select the single idea with the highest potential for market disruption.
Phase 2: Prototype Development (5 Hours): Utilizing the concept selected, your team must immediately transmute the idea into a functional, working prototype. This sprint emphasizes leveraging modern, low-code/no-code tools for speed and agility.
Phase 3: PMF Validation (1 Hour 30 Minutes): The final phase focuses on Product-Market Fit (PMF). Teams will be challenged to test and validate their Go-To-Market strategies using the prototype and prepare a compelling pitch based on initial traction data.
The Value Proposition & Recognition:
We've designed this hackathon to be a concentrated dose of the startup lifecycle and reward future potential:
Founder Mastery: Acquire essential skills in end-to-end venture building, from initial concept validation to developing a compelling investor-ready pitch deck.
Expert Vetting: Present your final solution to a panel of expert judges, mimicking the critical seed-stage pitch experience.
Exciting Prizes: Winners will receive prizes worth ₹5000 in the form of goodies and invaluable mentorship opportunities from our network of seasoned entrepreneurs and industry experts.
Incentivized Scaling: Top-tier teams secure automatic entry and 2-Month Free Access to the highly coveted School of Entrepreneurship, an exclusive founder incubation pipeline.
Validation: All participants will receive a formal Certificate of Participation, confirming your engagement in this intense disruptor crucible.
Seamless Onboarding Process:
Primary Registration: Secure your spot by registering exclusively via the Unstop platform.
Next step for completing your registration is to fill this
Google Form
Ecosystem Integration: Upon successful filling of the google form, you will be added to the dedicated communication channel on WhatsApp for real-time updates and logistical orchestration.

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Rewind And Recode (West Bengal)
Step into the world of innovation, creativity, and non-stop coding at the Rewind and Recode Hackathon, a premier National Level Hackathon hosted by the esteemed International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Bhubaneswar. This one-of-a-kind event brings together the best and brightest problem solvers, coders, and innovators from across India to tackle real-world challenges with disruptive technology and bold ideas.
Scheduled from 7th to 9th November 2025, Rewind and Recode isn’t just another hackathon — it’s a launchpad for student innovation, a battlefield of logic and creativity, and a celebration of India’s next-generation tech talent. With 24 hours of non-stop ideation, coding, collaboration, and execution, participants will compete head-to-head to build impactful, scalable, and solution-oriented tech projects.
Whether you're from a premier institute like IIT, NIT, IIIT or a passionate developer from any engineering college across India, if you're driven to create meaningful solutions, this is your stage. This hackathon invites teams from every corner of the country, ensuring a diverse pool of minds working across multiple themes — including healthcare, sustainability, agriculture, finance, education, cybersecurity, and more.
The problem statements will be released on 7th November at 8:00 AM IST, marking the beginning of the hackathon.
Teams will then engage in a high-intensity sprint — analyzing, brainstorming, coding, and pitching — all within a tightly packed 24-hour window.
The atmosphere will be buzzing with energy at the on-site venue of IIIT Bhubaneswar, as students code side-by-side with peers, under the mentorship of experienced tech experts and industry leaders.
But it’s not just about writing code. Rewind and Recode emphasizes end-to-end problem solving — identifying core pain points, proposing real-world solutions, justifying tech stacks, building intuitive UIs, and pitching like professionals. It’s a full-cycle product development experience compressed into 24 hours — intense, insightful, and insanely rewarding.
Registrations open in August 2025, and interested teams are advised to register early through the official portal. Once registered, teams will be eligible for further updates, problem statements, and participation in internal shortlisting (if applicable via respective institutions). Ensure your team is formed, registered, and ready before the hackathon begins!
What’s in it for you?
Besides exciting prizes, certificates, and recognition, you get a chance to:
Network with students and tech leaders from across India
Receive mentorship from professionals
Test your skills in a real-world, time-constrained setup
Strengthen your portfolio and technical credibility
This is not just a hackathon — it’s your chance to shine on the national stage.
So, gather your team. Register this August 2025.
And from 7th to 9th November, come to IIIT Bhubaneswar to Rewind what you know… and Recode what the world needs.
Let’s innovate. Let’s hustle. Let’s Hack.

Name: Code-O-lympics
Organizer: International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Bhubaneswar
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Code-O-lympics
Timeline:
Preliminary Round -
Online
- 20 October
Final Round -
Offline (IIIT Bhubaneswar)
- 8 November
Registration Deadline - 19 October
Team Composition:
Each team must consist of exactly 3 members.
No individual participation or teams with less than or more than 3 members are allowed.
All team members must be registered prior to the event. No changes to team members will be allowed once the event starts.
Preliminary Round Details:
The first round will be conducted entirely online.
All team members must participate in the contest individually.
The team’s evaluation will be based on the average score of all members.
Final Round Equipment Rules:
Teams are permitted to use a maximum of 2 laptops during the competition. A third laptop or any other electronic device (like tablets or phones) is strictly prohibited.
Teams are responsible for bringing their own laptops, chargers, and other necessary equipment.
Pre-installed coding environments (IDEs, compilers, etc.) are allowed, but the use of any pre-written code, templates, or libraries that are not part of the standard libraries provided by the competition is forbidden.
Parallel Contests and Scoring for Finals:
The team's final score will be the combined score from both contests. Scores from each contest will be added together to determine the team’s total score.
Teams should strategize effectively, as balanced performance across both contests will be crucial for maximizing the total score and ranking.
Usage of Paper and Writing Materials for Finals:
Each participant is allowed to use 2 paper sheets for rough work.
Teams must bring their own writing materials (pen/pencil, etc.). No extra sheets will be provided once the event starts.
Use of notebooks, books, or printed materials, aside from the allowed 2 sheets, is prohibited. Teams must dispose of any additional paper prior to the event.
Problem Set:
The problem set will consist of 8 coding problems divided into three levels of difficulty:
2 Easy problems
4 Medium problems
2 Hard problems
The problems are designed to test algorithmic thinking, data structure implementation, and problem-solving skills.
Each problem will have detailed descriptions, sample test cases, and constraints.
Competition Duration:
The event will run for 3 hours continuously.
No extra time will be given for setup or troubleshooting unless a technical failure occurs, in which case the organizing team will determine if additional time should be allotted.
Teams are advised to manage their time wisely, ensuring a balance between solving problems of different difficulty levels.
Judging Criteria:
The event follows a score-and-time-based system.
Teams are ranked based on the number of problems solved.
In the case of a tie (teams solving the same number of problems), the team that solved the problems in the least amount of time (including penalties for wrong submissions) will be ranked higher.
Only correct solutions that pass all the given test cases for a problem will be considered as fully solved.
In the case of a tie-breaker, further criteria (such as performance in harder problems) may be applied to decide the winner.
Collaboration and Code of Conduct:
All team members are allowed to discuss the problems within the team, but collaboration with other teams or external sources is strictly prohibited.
Any form of cheating or rule violation (e.g., use of unauthorized devices, internet access, or discussing problems with others outside the team) will result in immediate disqualification.
Teams are expected to uphold the spirit of fair play and competition.
Allowed Programming Languages:
Participants may use any of the standard programming languages supported by the competition platform, which typically include:
C, C++, Java, Python, and others as specified.
Teams should be familiar with the syntax and capabilities of their chosen languages prior to the event, as there will be no provision for tutorials or assistance on language-specific issues.
Penalty for Wrong Submissions:
Each wrong submission will result in a time penalty of 2 minutes.
The time penalty could influence the final ranking, as teams with more wrong submissions may end up with higher completion times, even if they solve the same number of problems as another team.
To avoid excessive penalties, teams are advised to carefully review their code and test cases before making a submission.

Eligibility Criteria:

Registration Process:

Problem Statement:
This event challenges participants to develop innovative, lightweight, and resource-efficient High-Performance Computing (HPC) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) models that can address critical social welfare problems. The goal is to create powerful yet sustainable solutions that work even in resource-constrained environments. Teams can choose to make project on problem statements from the following domains:
AI for Legal Domains
AI for Governance
AI for Healthcare
AI for Women Safety
AI for Disaster Management
A single team can opt for a single Problem Statement ONLY.
Competition Structure:
The event comprises three rounds:
Registration Deadline: 7th October 2025
Round 1 (Online): All registered teams will compete and 150 teams will advance to the next round. Results will be declared on 13th October 2025.

Everything you need to know about
CodeBlitz(Mini Codevita)
Welcome to the Mini CodeVita Challenge, an exclusive coding competition designed to bring together enthusiastic programmers, logical thinkers, and tech innovators on a single platform. Inspired by top coding contests, this event aims to provide participants with a real-time problem-solving experience that will not only sharpen their technical skills but also prepare them for larger platforms like TCS CodeVita, Hackathons, and global coding marathons.
The Mini CodeVita is more than just a contest—it is a journey that allows students and early professionals to explore their programming potential, improve algorithmic thinking, and develop the persistence required in real-world problem solving. Whether you are a beginner who wants to test your skills or an advanced coder aiming to compete with the best, this challenge is for you.

Programming Fundamentals – Strong command over languages like C, C++, Java, Python, or JavaScript.
Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA) – Application of arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, graphs, hashing, dynamic programming, and greedy methods.
Mathematical & Logical Thinking – Ability to solve mathematical problems, puzzles, and logical reasoning challenges using code.
Problem-Solving Mindset – Breaking down complex problems into manageable sub-problems, writing efficient solutions, and optimizing code.
Debugging & Optimization – Writing clean, bug-free, and time-efficient code suitable for large inputs.
Competitive Programming Skills – Developing accuracy, speed, and adaptability under time-bound conditions.
Teamwork & Collaboration (if team participation is enabled) – Sharing ideas, brainstorming, and combining strengths for innovative problem solving.

Name: Terraquest 2K25: Hackathon for a Sustainable Future
Organizer: Sri Sairam College of Engineering Bangalore
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Terraquest 2K25: Hackathon for a Sustainable Future
Terraquest 2K25 is a hands-on hackathon organized by Sri Sairam College of Engineering, where students and innovators work together to solve important environmental and social problems. The theme is based on the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), which are global targets for improving our world. This event invites anyone who cares about making a real difference to join and develop creative solutions, either by choosing from suggested challenges or proposing their own ideas—provided a mentor approves them for relevance and practicality.
What Can Participants Work On?
Participants can form teams and choose problems from the following SDG focus areas:
SDG 3: Good Health and Well-being
Make wearable or IoT devices that monitor things like heart rate and oxygen levels for rural areas.
Build apps for tracking health conditions, reporting diseases, or reminding people to take their medicine.
SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation
Create smart devices and apps to monitor water quality in villages.
Build solutions to report water leaks or save water.
Design affordable rainwater collection systems.
SDG 7: Affordable and Clean Energy
Invent platforms for trading solar electricity within communities.
Build apps to analyze energy use and suggest improvements.
Develop portable solar-powered devices.
SDG 13: Climate Action
Design tools or dashboards to monitor air pollution or greenhouse gases.
Make apps for warning about extreme weather.
Create easy-to-understand data visuals for government use.
SDG 15: Life on Land
Build drones to track forests and stop illegal logging.
Make AR/VR tools to teach about wildlife and the environment.
Develop systems to record pollution affecting plants and animals.
Unique Features:
Teams can also come up with their own original problems and solutions, as long as they fit the hackathon’s SDG themes and get approval from organizers.
Mentors and organizers review ideas to make sure they are important, doable within the hackathon’s timeframe, and truly impactful.
If an idea isn’t approved, teams get feedback to help them improve and resubmit.
What Happens at the Event?
The hackathon is a two-day, hands-on event held in person at the college.
Day 1: Kick-off session, idea building, code sprints, judging rounds, and workshops about sustainability.
Day 2: Coding, more judging, and final project presentations, ending with awards and networking.
Who Should Join?
Students, engineers, programmers, designers, and anyone motivated to use technology for good are welcome.
The event is especially great for people with skills in coding, hardware, data analysis, AI, or environmental science.
How are Solutions Judged?
Projects will be evaluated on:
Originality and creative thinking.
How realistic and doable the idea is, especially within the hackathon’s short time.
Technical strength and thoroughness.
Real-world impact and relevance to the chosen SDG.
How well teams explain and present their solutions.
Prizes:
1st Prize: ₹10,000 (Hardware + Software)
2nd Prize: ₹5,000 (Hardware + Software)
Why Join TERRAQUEST 2K25?
TERRAQUEST 2K25 gives participants a chance to:
Gain real technical and teamwork experience.
Learn from experts and mentors.
Network with people passionate about sustainability.
Make a positive impact in the world.
Be a part of TERRAQUEST 2K25 to help create a more sustainable and fair future using innovation and technology.
Registration:
Fee: ₹250 per head (Total 1000 per team)

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Code of Honour 2.0
Team Shunya, the Official Mathematics Club of PES University, Ring Road Campus, Bengaluru, Karnataka, along with Team Samarpana, a student run initiative for the martyr familes, presents
CODE OF HONOUR 2.0, second iteration of our flagship hackathon for the Odd semester of the academic year 2025-26, to nurture student interest in Mathematics, Coding and Cybersecurity beyond academics and to channel these skills toward strengthening our armed forces.
Code of Honour 2.0 is a state-level coding event for university students across Karnataka.
The event will have two parts:
Round 1 (Online Selection): Teams will take part in a Capture The Flag (CTF) challenge. Alongside the CTF, teams will also propose an innovative idea under the given tracks and submit a presentation outlining their solution. Both their performance in the CTF and the originality of their idea will be considered for selection.
Round 2 (On-Campus Hackathon): The top 36 teams will be invited for the offline round, where they will further develop their ideas into projects under the five defense-related tracks. Projects should leverage applied mathematics and coding to enhance security, communication resilience, data analysis, and robotics for defense applications.
The team with the most impactful solution or model will be awarded.
Tracks:
Machine Learning/Generative AI in Operational technology for Military Intelligence
Robotics/Surveillance Technology
Cybersecurity for Military Intelligence
DevTools
Open Innovation
Rules:
A team should comprise of a minimum of 3 and a maximum of 4
members.
Any team found to involve themselves in any kind of malpractice/plagiarism (Including flag sharing with other teams) will be directly disqualified without any further questions.
A team must consist of participants belonging to the same college/university.
Participants should present their unique idea with a PPT presentation.
Copying any ideas from other teams is strictly prohibited.
Decisions taken by the judges are final. No further clarifications would be entertained.
Judging will be based on the below mentioned parameters:
Novelty
Creativity
Implementation
Scalability

Pick a Track: Select one of the given tracks to focus your solution.

Choose a Tech Stack: Identify the tools, frameworks, and technologies you will use to build your solution.
Prepare a 4-Pager PPT Submission: Summarize your track, problem statement, proposed solution, and tech stack in a concise presentation (maximum 4 slides).

Name: Adopt a Planet - CSR Ideathon
Organizer: Chandigarh Univeristy
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Adopt a Planet - CSR Ideathon
Adopt a Planet – CSR Ideathon is a unique interstellar-themed innovation challenge where students step into the role of CSR leaders for fictional planets facing Earth-like crises such as climate change, food insecurity, energy shortages, inequality, or environmental degradation.
The mission: design sustainable, creative, and feasible CSR strategies to rescue the adopted planet while reflecting solutions for today’s real-world global challenges.
Eligibility & Team Guidelines:
Open to all Undergraduate (UG) and Postgraduate (PG) students across disciplines
Team size: 2–4 members
Inter-college participation: Allowed (if permitted by university)
Inter-specialization teams: Allowed (students from different streams can collaborate)
A student can participate in only one team
All team members must be present throughout the event
Event Structure & Process (16th October 2025 | Chandigarh University):
Round 1 – Problem Analysis (Brainstorming):
Teams adopt a random Planet Profile Card describing a crisis scenario
Analyze the problem, map it to relevant UN SDGs, and brainstorm innovative solutions
Deliverable: 2-minute checkpoint pitch (problem + initial ideas)
Duration: 60 minutes (prep + pitch)
Round 2 – Solution Development (Poster/Slide Creation):
Refine ideas into structured CSR strategies
Prepare a Poster or PPT covering: identified problem, proposed solution, SDG alignment, feasibility, and sustainability impact
Duration: 2 hours (prep)
Final Round – CSR Pitch Presentation:
5-minute pitch + 2-minute Q&A before the jury
Evaluation based on creativity, feasibility, impact, SDG alignment, and clarity
Rules & Guidelines:
All ideas must be original; plagiarism or reuse of previous projects leads to disqualification
Each solution must align with at least one UN Sustainable Development Goal (SDG)
Time limits must be followed:
2 minutes (checkpoint pitch)
5 minutes (final pitch) + 2 minutes Q&A
One solution per team
All members must remain present throughout
Use of AI tools, graphics, or design software is allowed, but originality is key
Judges’ decisions are final and binding
Teams must demonstrate feasibility, scalability, and sustainability
Cross-department collaboration is encouraged
Respectful behavior and adherence to university guidelines is mandatory
Judging Criteria:
Creativity & Originality – 25%
Feasibility & Impact – 25%
Alignment with UN SDGs – 20%
Presentation & Clarity – 20%
Teamwork & Engagement – 10%
Rewards & Recognition:
Galactic Changemaker – Best Overall Solution
Most Creative Rescue Plan
Best Sustainable Impact Idea
Certificates of Participation titled CSR Astronaut for all participants
Special recognition tags: Planet Protector and Ambassador of Lumina-

Capture the Flag
Event Overview:
The 2025 RVU CTF is a Capture the Flag (CTF) competition designed to test problem-solving, logical thinking, and cybersecurity skills
Suitable for both beginners and experienced CTF players
Focuses on challenges, teamwork, and high-energy competition
Event Format:

Name: GameDraft
Organizer: International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Hyderabad
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
GameDraft
Get ready to dive into the world of game design at Game Draft, an exhilarating event hosted by The Gaming Club at IIIT Hyderabad! Join us for a 2-hour hybrid workshop to master the basics of game design and discover the power of a Game Design Document (GDD). Then, unleash your creativity in a thrilling 12-hour hackathon, where you’ll craft your own GDD and bring your game ideas to life!
Join our
Whatsapp group
for doubts and communication: https://chat.whatsapp.com/DL3Bm1qjS4OHhIlgW3DM5u
Guidelines:
Eligibility-
Open to all undergraduate and postgraduate students from any recognized institution, as well as early-career professionals and game design enthusiasts aged 18 and above. Participants must have basic familiarity with creative tools like Google Docs, Miro, or similar for collaborative design (no prior coding required). Teams must register in advance, and individual participants can join or form teams on-site/online.
Inter-college team members allowed-
Yes, teams can include members from different colleges to encourage diverse perspectives and interdisciplinary collaboration.
Inter-specialization team members allowed-
Yes, teams can mix members from various fields (e.g., computer science, arts, business, psychology) to foster innovative game ideas that blend technical, narrative, and user-experience elements.
During the hackathon, teams will receive 1 main problem statement: "Design a GDD for an original game concept addressing a real-world theme (e.g., sustainability, mental health, or cultural heritage)." No additional sub-questions; focus is on open-ended ideation and documentation.
Duration of the rounds-
Workshop round: 2 hours (introductory session with lectures and Q&A). The video for the workshop will be provided to our online participants.
Hackathon round: 12 hours (continuous ideation, design, and submission phase).
Rules:
Teams can consist of 1-3 members maximum; solo participation is allowed but encouraged to team up for richer ideas.
All work must be original; plagiarism in GDD content (e.g., copying mechanics from existing games without credit) results in disqualification.
Use of AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT for brainstorming) is permitted but must be disclosed in the GDD's appendix, with a limit of 20% of total content generated by AI.
Submissions must be a complete GDD (5-7 pages, including sections on concept, mechanics, story, assets, and monetization) uploaded on unstop by the hackathon deadline.
Winners announced within 1 week post-event.
No prototypes required, focus is on design documentation only.
Code of conduct:
Respectful collaboration; harassment or disruptive behavior leads to voiding of registration at any stage.

Name: Clash of Code
Organizer: Central University of Jammu
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Clash of Code
Clash of Code is a two-round data structures and algorithms (DSA) competition designed to challenge and showcase the coding prowess of undergraduate students. This event provides a platform for participants to solve complex problems, apply their knowledge of fundamental algorithms and data structures, and compete for exciting prizes and recognition.
Guidelines:
Eligibility: The competition is open to all undergraduate students.
Format: The competition will be held online and consists of two rounds.
Round 1: This is a preliminary round featuring 50 MCQ-based questions to evaluate general computer knowledge. Participants must qualify in this round to proceed.
Round 2: This is the main coding round with 3 DSA-based problems. Only those who qualify from Round 1 will be eligible for this round.
Duration: The total competition will last for 3 hours.
Round 1: 1 hour.
Round 2: 2 hours.
Team Composition: The competition is for individual participants only.
Rules:
Scoring:
Round 1: Each correct answer earns +2 points, while each incorrect answer results in a -0.5 point penalty.
Round 2: Each problem has a maximum score. The score is awarded based on the number of test cases your code passes. Partial scoring is enabled.
Tie-Breaker: In the event of a tie in the final scores, the participant who solved the problems in the shortest total time will be ranked higher.
Code Submissions: Participants can use any standard programming language supported by the competition platform (e.g., C++, Java, Python). A full list of supported languages will be provided to all participants beforehand.
Cheating: Sharing code or solutions with other individuals is strictly prohibited. Any form of plagiarism or academic misconduct will result in immediate disqualification. Participants are allowed to use standard libraries and functions available in their chosen programming language.
Technical Requirements: Participants are responsible for their own hardware and a stable internet connection. No extra time will be granted for any technical issues on the participant's end.
Disqualification: Organizers reserve the right to disqualify any participant who violates the rules. All decisions made by the organizing committee are final.
Updates: All official communications, including important announcements and clarifications, will be made through email and the WhatsApp group. Participants are advised to check for updates regularly.
We look forward to seeing you at the "Clash of Code" and wish you the best of luck!
