Name: SAARTHI 2025
Organizer: Graphic Era Hill University (GEHU), Dehradun Campus
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
SAARTHI 2025
Innovating for Inclusion | National Hackathon on Disability-Centric Solutions
SAARTHI Hackathon 2025 is a 24-hour national-level hackathon dedicated to innovating for disability empowerment and inclusion, hosted at Graphic Era Hill University, Dehradun.
Inspired by its name “SAARTHI” (meaning companion or guide), the hackathon’s vision is to bring together the brightest student innovators from across the country to design and develop impactful solutions for people with disabilities—enabling a more inclusive and accessible tomorrow.
Event Details
Duration:
24 hours of focused innovation
Dates:
8th – 9th November 2025
Venue:
Graphic Era Hill University, Dehradun
Eligibility:
Open to all Undergraduate & Postgraduate students from diverse domains including B.Tech, BCA, MCA, BBA, MBA, B.Sc., B.Com, etc.
What’s Special in SAARTHI Hackathon 2025?
This year, SAARTHI focuses exclusively on disability-centric challenges:
Assistive Technology Focus:
Real-world problem statements addressing mobility, communication, healthcare, accessibility, and digital inclusion for specially-abled communities
Innovation with Impact:
Solutions must be affordable, scalable, and sustainable
Prizes & Goodies Worth ₹1 Lakhs+:
Includes incubation support and mentorship to turn ideas into impactful startups
Problem Statement Tracks
Participants can choose from 10 innovation tracks:
Mobility & Navigation Tech
– IoT smart canes, automated wheelchairs, haptic bands with AI obstacle detection and AR/VR navigation
Inclusive Education Tools
– Braille displays, tactile tablets, 3D learning aids powered by AI personalization and immersive AR/VR
Visual, Hearing & Cognitive Accessibility
– High-contrast modes, dyslexia-friendly layouts, simplified content, and plain language summaries
Core Web Accessibility & Assistive Tech Integration
– WCAG-compliant websites with keyboard navigation, ARIA roles, and screen reader support
IoT for Accessibility
– Smart homes, voice-controlled appliances, sensor alerts, and remote monitoring for independent living
Multilingual & Cultural Inclusivity
– Language toggles, RTL support, and local-language TTS/voice commands for global accessibility
Healthcare & Rehabilitation Technologies
– Smart prosthetics, exoskeletons, health trackers with AI physiotherapy and VR rehab apps
Workplace Accessibility
– Adaptive hardware and inclusive software for job matching, testing, and workplace integration
Policy, Awareness & Community Solutions
– Advocacy platforms, accessibility audit tools, and volunteer networks for social impact
Open Innovation for Disabilities & Accessibility
– Any creative, inclusive solution that empowers persons with disabilities
Team Details
Team Size:
2 – 4 members
Registration Fee:
Free for Round 1. A nominal fee will be applicable for shortlisted teams in Round 2.
Certificates:
Provided to all participants
Accommodation & Meals:
Provided for all participants
Why Participate?
SAARTHI Hackathon 2025 is not just a hackathon - it’s a movement for inclusion.
By focusing on disability-centered innovations, it encourages students to think empathetically, act inclusively, and innovate responsibly.
Benefits include:
Direct mentorship from experts in assistive tech & healthcare
Incubation opportunities for impactful projects

Name: Code the Cosmos
Organizer: Indian Institute of Technology, Indian School of Mines (IIT ISM), Dhanbad
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Code the Cosmos
Event Structure:
Decoding the Stars:
At this stage, participants face puzzles inspired by astronomy and astrophysics. Star maps may hide coordinates, orbital paths may mask keys, and constellations may need to be decoded into meaningful data. This round blends scientific reasoning with cryptographic thinking, requiring you to see order in cosmic chaos.
Establishing Contact:
Communication with the satellite is fragmented. Broken radio signals, distorted spectrograms, and scrambled waveforms must be untangled to uncover hidden keys. Participants will learn to probe corrupted transmissions, filter noise, and reconstruct messages. The challenge lies in distinguishing signal from interference, just as the captain intended.
Inside the Satellite Core:
Once communication is restored, the real work begins. The satellite’s digital core is in ruins, filled with corrupted files, abandoned logs, and fragments of code. Hidden among them are pieces of the trail left behind by the captain. Competitors must use forensics skills to sift through the remains, locate essential fragments, and reconstruct meaning from partial data.
The Door Guard:
Here lies the final barrier before control can be reclaimed. The Zorgons have reinforced the satellite’s systems with alien encryption. Participants must identify weaknesses, exploit cryptographic flaws, and bypass advanced defenses. Each layer cracked brings them closer to the command center, but every failed attempt risks alerting the alien presence.
Restoring Control:
The last level is the culmination of all efforts. Once inside, participants must reassemble the captain’s master key and use it to override Zorgon control. Success means full system restoration and the satellite’s safe return. Failure leaves it drifting deeper into the void, permanently lost.
Rules of Engagement:
Team Format:
Teams may consist of
1–3 participants
. Collaboration within the team is encouraged, but cross-team assistance is prohibited.
Scoring:
Jeopardy-style format with a
live leaderboard
. Points are awarded based on challenge difficulty, and
speed plus accuracy
determine your final ranking.
Multiple Attempts:
Challenges may be attempted
multiple times
within the event window. Only correct submissions earn points.
Fair Play:
Brute-force attacks beyond intended scope are not allowed.
Automated tools or scripts are forbidden unless explicitly permitted.
Outside help, leaks, or collaboration between separate teams is strictly prohibited.
Misconduct Rules:
Any attempt to disrupt the event infrastructure will result in
instant disqualification
.
Sharing solutions
with other teams during the event is strictly forbidden.
Plagiarism
or reliance on unfair means will lead to removal from the competition
Do register your team on astc.live . The event will be starting at 4p.m. today.

Name: Rewind And Recode (Delhi & Haryana)
Organizer: International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Bhubaneswar
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Rewind And Recode (Delhi & Haryana)
Only join if your College  belongs to the Delhi or Haryana  State Region
Step into the world of innovation, creativity, and non-stop coding at the Rewind and Recode Hackathon, a premier National Level Hackathon hosted by the esteemed International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Bhubaneswar. This one-of-a-kind event brings together the best and brightest problem solvers, coders, and innovators from across India to tackle real-world challenges with disruptive technology and bold ideas.
Scheduled from 7th to 9th November 2025, Rewind and Recode isn’t just another hackathon — it’s a launchpad for student innovation, a battlefield of logic and creativity, and a celebration of India’s next-generation tech talent. With 24 hours of non-stop ideation, coding, collaboration, and execution, participants will compete head-to-head to build impactful, scalable, and solution-oriented tech projects.
Whether you're from a premier institute like IIT, NIT, IIIT or a passionate developer from any engineering college across India, if you're driven to create meaningful solutions, this is your stage. This hackathon invites teams from every corner of the country, ensuring a diverse pool of minds working across multiple themes — including healthcare, sustainability, agriculture, finance, education, cybersecurity, and more.
The problem statements will be released on 7th November at 8:00 AM IST, marking the beginning of the hackathon. Teams will then engage in a high-intensity sprint — analyzing, brainstorming, coding, and pitching — all within a tightly packed 24-hour window. The atmosphere will be buzzing with energy at the on-site venue of IIIT Bhubaneswar, as students code side-by-side with peers, under the mentorship of experienced tech experts and industry leaders.
But it’s not just about writing code. Rewind and Recode emphasizes end-to-end problem solving — identifying core pain points, proposing real-world solutions, justifying tech stacks, building intuitive UIs, and pitching like professionals. It’s a full-cycle product development experience compressed into 24 hours — intense, insightful, and insanely rewarding.
Registrations open in August 2025, and interested teams are advised to register early through the official portal. Once registered, teams will be eligible for further updates, problem statements, and participation in internal shortlisting (if applicable via respective institutions). Ensure your team is formed, registered, and ready before the hackathon begins!
What’s in it for you?
Besides exciting prizes, certificates, and recognition, you get a chance to:
Network with students and tech leaders from across India
Receive mentorship from professionals
Test your skills in a real-world, time-constrained setup
Strengthen your portfolio and technical credibility
This is not just a hackathon — it’s your chance to shine on the national stage.
So, gather your team. Register this August 2025.
And from 7th to 9th November, come to IIIT Bhubaneswar to Rewind what you know… and Recode what the world needs.
Let’s innovate. Let’s hustle. Let’s Hack.

About the Competition:
Hack the future with AI, quantum computing, and data innovation

UST

The Mexico edition invites young innovators to apply advanced technologies—including AI, Quantum Computing, and Intelligent Data Ecosystems—in a multi-stage competition that leads to a global finale alongside regional winners from around the world.
D3 theme:
Shaping new frontiers – Data, Intelligence and Quantum.

Name: Capture the Flag
Organizer: NIIT University
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Capture the Flag
Capture The Flag (CTF), held in association with NU’s CyberNutica Cybersecurity Club, is an exciting event where participants use their critical thinking and problem-solving abilities to uncover hidden "flags" buried inside vulnerable systems. Each solved challenge brings points, pushing participants to test their skills in cryptography, web exploitation, reverse engineering, and more.
Long Description / Overview:
Capture The Flag (CTF) is one of the most thrilling competitions in the cybersecurity world, and this event brings that same energy and challenge to NU. The goal of the event is simple but challenging: find hidden pieces of information called “flags” by solving a variety of tasks that simulate real-world security problems. These flags are unique strings of text embedded in different types of challenges.
Each challenge is carefully designed to test participants’ knowledge of cybersecurity domains such as cryptography, digital forensics, web vulnerabilities, and reverse engineering. Challenges vary in difficulty, and completing them rewards participants with corresponding points—the harder the challenge, the higher the points.
Participants submit their discovered flags to the competition platform, which automatically validates correctness and awards points instantly. The competition leaderboard updates live, adding an exciting layer of urgency and strategy. In the end, the participant who collects the most points in the least amount of time will be declared the CTF Champion.
This event is not only a test of technical expertise but also of creativity, persistence, and logical problem-solving. CTF pushes participants to think like real hackers—not maliciously, but with the mindset of uncovering vulnerabilities and securing systems. It’s an opportunity to dive deep into the world of cybersecurity and sharpen problem-solving skills in a gamified, competitive environment.
Event Structure:
This will be a 24-hour online event.
Registered participants will receive competition access links via email.
The competition platform will host all challenges in different categories such as Cryptography, Web Exploitation, and Reverse Engineering.
Live leaderboards will display participant scores and rankings.
Challenge Categories:
Cryptography – Tasks requiring participants to decrypt secret messages, decode encoded strings, or solve puzzles involving data encryption methods.
Web Exploitation – Identify and exploit vulnerabilities in simulated web applications to extract hidden flags.
Reverse Engineering – Analyze compiled programs, disassemble code, and uncover flags hidden in binary executables.
Rules and Regulations:
This is a single-round event.
Individual Participation Only – No teams are allowed.
Leaderboards are ranked based on total points. In case of a tie, the participant who submitted earlier will rank higher.
Participants must not share flags or solutions during the contest. Any collaboration leads to disqualification.
Any attempt to disrupt the platform or violate the rules will result in immediate disqualification.
All decisions regarding eligibility, authenticity, and final judgment will rest with the organizing committee.
Note:
Information about cash prize and goodie will be available on the siNUsoid website mentioned
Important Dates: Registration Deadline 04 Nov 25, 06:30 PM CUT

Name: Code Jam
Organizer: NIIT University
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Code Jam
Put your programming and logic skills to the test in this solo coding challenge. Solve problems of varying difficulty and climb the leaderboard by writing efficient, smart solutions.
Overview / Long Description:
CodeClash is an exciting individual coding competition designed to push participants’ problem-solving and programming abilities to the limit. In this event, participants will face a set of coding challenges that range from beginner-friendly warmups to advanced-level puzzles that require sharp logical reasoning and efficient solutions.
Unlike typical coding practice sessions, CodeClash emphasizes not only correctness but also the efficiency and optimization of solutions. Participants must carefully evaluate problem statements, design suitable approaches, and implement them within the given time. The competition will take place in a HackerRank contest format, ensuring fairness, transparency, and a structured leaderboard system.
Each problem will be assigned points based on its difficulty level. Beginners can rack up points by solving simpler problems, while experienced coders can aim for the high-value, complex ones. Ultimately, the leaderboard will reflect not only who solves the most problems, but who solves them with accuracy and efficiency. This creates a balanced environment where both speed and strategic problem selection matter.
The competition serves as the perfect platform for programmers who want to showcase their abilities in a real, time-bound coding environment while competing against peers. Whether you are an experienced coder or a passionate learner, CodeClash provides the right stage to test your logic, programming fluency, and ability to think under pressure.
Event Structure:
Format: Individual participation only.
Platform: HackerRank contest environment (with pre-set problem statements).
Duration: Fixed time window where participants solve as many problems as possible.
Problem Range: Questions will cover a wide spectrum—from beginner problems (loops, arrays, strings) to advanced ones (dynamic programming, graph theory, optimization).
Scoring: Each problem carries points based on difficulty level. More problems solved = more points earned. The leaderboard will update in real-time.
Rules and Regulations:
The event will be conducted offline in a supervised environment.
Participation is strictly individual; no teams or collaborations are allowed.
Problems must be solved independently—any form of discussion, copying, or external help will result in immediate disqualification.
Participants may only use the resources provided on the contest platform. External compilers, internet browsing, or unauthorized materials are prohibited.
Any attempt to disrupt the competition, manipulate the platform, or bypass rules will lead to disqualification.
All decisions regarding eligibility, authenticity, and final judgment rest solely with the judges and organizers.
Judges’ decisions are final and binding.
Note:
All the information about the cash prize and goodies will be available on the website mentioned

Name: Codsole XP
Organizer: NIIT University
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Codsole XP
A fast-paced game jam where creativity meets AI. Build a game using any engine, enhance it with AI-generated assets, and showcase your story, design, or mechanics.
Long Description:
Codsole XP is a unique creative game jam designed to celebrate the fusion of human imagination and artificial intelligence. The event challenges participants to design and develop a playable game around a surprise theme that will only be revealed a week before the final submission deadline. Unlike traditional game jams, Codsole XP emphasizes how AI tools can enrich storytelling, aesthetics, and world-building, while keeping the core coding and gameplay logic firmly in the hands of the participants.
This event provides a space for developers, designers, artists, and storytellers to experiment with the latest AI-assisted workflows. Whether it’s using AI to generate atmospheric music, crafting dialogue through language models, or creating textures and assets with image-generation tools, participants will experience firsthand how AI can serve as a powerful creative collaborator. At the same time, they will have the opportunity to demonstrate their own technical skills, originality, and design thinking through the development of functional game prototypes.
Codsole XP is not just a competition but a creative playground where participants push the boundaries of what is possible in modern game development. By combining game mechanics, narrative design, art direction, and AI innovation, each team will deliver a unique project that reflects the future of interactive entertainment.
Timeline:
Theme Release: One week prior to the final submission.
Development Window: 7 days to conceptualize, build, and finalize the game.
Structure:
Platform: Hybrid (both online and offline submissions allowed).
Team Size: Solo developers or teams of 2–4 members.
Presentation Round: Each team will present their game to the judging panel. Presentations must cover gameplay demonstration, creative process, and AI usage in the project.
Rules and Regulations:
Teams must develop a playable game prototype during the 7-day jam window.
All game logic and coding must be implemented manually by the participants. Use of AI for the core logic or gameplay systems is strictly prohibited.
AI tools may be used for supporting elements such as world building, asset generation, dialogues, level design, visuals, and audio.
The submitted game must be functional and hosted either on itch.io or provided as a downloadable zip file.
If itch.io hosting is not possible, participants must directly provide the packaged zip.
Every submission must include a short explanation of AI usage, either in the demo or within the documentation.
Any violation of rules or attempts at plagiarism will lead to disqualification.
Judges’ decisions will be final and binding.

For Game Development:
Unity (C# or BOLT)

Godot
GDevelop

Name: Predictive Precision
Organizer: NIIT University
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Predictive Precision
Join Predictive Precision, the ultimate machine learning challenge where your team’s data analysis and model training skills will be put to the test! Build robust models on real-world datasets, showcase your precision, and compete for the top spot.
Long Description & Overview:
Predictive Precision is a data-driven machine learning competition designed for problem-solvers, innovators, and technology enthusiasts. The challenge tests a team’s ability to perform end-to-end machine learning workflows—from understanding raw datasets to deploying production-ready models.
Participants will receive real-world datasets and a problem statement carefully curated by the organizers. Teams must dive deep into data analysis, uncover patterns, and train predictive models that demonstrate accuracy, efficiency, and creativity. This is not just about coding—it’s about telling a complete story with data.
Judging will be based on:
Model Accuracy & Performance
Depth of Data Analysis
Clarity of Approach & Justification
Documentation Quality
Final Presentation & Demo
The event mirrors real-world data science challenges, where participants must not only build powerful models but also explain their approach clearly and convincingly to both technical and non-technical audiences. This makes Predictive Precision a platform to showcase teamwork, analytical thinking, communication skills, and technical expertise.
Event Structure:
The theme for the event will be released three days prior to the competition, allowing participants time to familiarize themselves with the context. The dataset and problem statement will be provided by the organizers.
Each team will present an 8–10 minute solution walkthrough, followed by a 5–10 minute Q&A session with the judging panel. Teams may use any programming language of their choice—Python, R, Julia, or even MATLAB—making the event inclusive and flexible.
Phase 1: Data Analysis & Preprocessing
Receive the dataset and problem statement (based on Kaggle datasets).
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA).
Handle missing values, outliers, and apply feature engineering.
Summarize key insights and justify preprocessing steps.
Phase 2: Model Building & Training
Train at least two different algorithms and compare their performance.
Evaluate using relevant metrics such as accuracy, F1-score, RMSE, or AUC.
Optimize hyperparameters for better results.
Justify the choice of your final model.
Phase 3: Deployment
Deploy your trained model to accept user input and generate predictions.
Document methodology, analysis, and results thoroughly.
Submit a working HTML export with visible outputs.
Upload code and documentation to a public GitHub repository with a detailed README.
Demo deployment options:
Flask/FastAPI + HTML/CSS/JS
Streamlit/Gradio for rapid prototyping
Hosting options: HuggingFace Spaces, Heroku, or Vercel.
Phase 4: Front-End (Optional for Bonus Points)
Create a simple user interface that allows data upload and displays results.
Highlight the workflow: dataset → model → predictions.
Bonus marks awarded for effective usability and polished design.
Submission Requirements
Working HTML file (exported notebook with outputs).
GitHub repository link with all code, documentation, and a structured README.
Final Presentation (5–7 minutes) explaining analysis, methodology, model performance, and deployment.
Rules:
Team Size: 3–5 members.
All submissions must be original and thoroughly documented.
Plagiarism in any form will lead to disqualification.
A complete solution (EDA, model, deployment, and presentation) is mandatory for scoring.
In case of a tie-breaker, evaluation will prioritize clarity, documentation quality, and early submission.
Use of AI tools for conceptual understanding is allowed, but the final code and models must be original. Submissions will undergo authenticity checks.

Name: Robo Soccer
Organizer: NIIT University
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Robo Soccer
The primary goal of the game is simple yet exciting: score more goals than the opposing team within a set time limit using custom-built robots.
Overview / Long Description:
Robo Soccer is an electrifying event that brings together the thrill of football and the innovation of robotics. The objective is for participants to build two manually operated robots capable of competing in a miniature football arena. This arena replicates a rectangular soccer field, scaled down for robots, where strategy, control, and quick decision-making matter just as much as raw speed and power.
The event is designed to challenge teams to engineer robots that can dribble, push, and maneuver the ball effectively, while maintaining stability and control. It is not only a test of robotics knowledge but also of teamwork, as every successful play requires coordination between two bots operated by different team members.
The excitement mirrors that of real football—fast-paced movement, quick passes, strong defenses, and clever goals. By blending the world’s most celebrated sport with cutting-edge robotics technology, Robo Soccer ensures a thrilling, competitive, and memorable experience for both participants and spectators. At the end of each match, the team with the most goals is declared the winner. If teams are tied, extra time and penalty shootouts bring in added drama, just like in professional football.
Rules & Regulations:
Team Formation: Each team can have a maximum of 5 members. All members must belong to a registered organization, college, or university.
Match Format: Matches are played in a 2 vs 2 format (two robots per team).
Match Duration: A standard match lasts 7 minutes, divided into two halves of 3.5 minutes each.
Decider Rounds:
If the match is tied after regulation time, a 1-minute decider match will be played.
If still tied, the winner will be decided through a penalty shootout.
Timeouts: Each team is allowed two technical timeouts lasting between 30 and 90 seconds.
Ball Stagnation Rule: If the ball is stationary for more than 15 seconds, both robots will be reset to the center of the field and the game will resume.
Prohibited Mechanisms:
Robots are not allowed to grab or hold the ball.
Use of fans, suction, or wind-based setups for ball control is strictly prohibited.
Substitutions: Teams may substitute members between rounds or during technical timeouts.
Fair Play: Any attempt to damage the opponent’s robot, misconduct, or unsportsmanlike behavior will lead to disqualification.
Final Authority: The decision of the event coordinators and jury will be final and binding.
Bot Specifications:
The robot must fit inside a box measuring 35cm x 35cm x 10cm. A 7% tolerance is allowed in dimensions and weight. Violating this leads to immediate disqualification.
The bot’s total weight, including batteries and all components, must not exceed 3 kg.
Power supply must be an on-board battery with a maximum of 12V.
Wireless control only is permitted; wired or external power supplies are not allowed.
Dangerous or unstable power sources will be banned.
Bots will be thoroughly inspected before the event to ensure compliance. Any bot not meeting requirements will not be allowed to participate.
Team Guidelines:
Teams should distribute roles among members, such as bot design, driving, repair, and strategy.
All robots must be built exclusively by team members. Outsourcing or use of ready-made robots is prohibited.
Teams should be prepared with spare parts, tools, and batteries to handle unexpected failures during the event.
Substitutions within a team’s lineup are permitted only as per the rules stated above.
Event Highlights:
Robo Soccer is not just a game—it’s an opportunity to showcase creativity, engineering skills, and team coordination. Participants get the chance to test their innovations in a live competition that simulates the adrenaline and energy of real football matches. With roaring cheers, close finishes, and high-pressure penalty shootouts, the Robo Soccer arena guarantees unforgettable moments for everyone involved.

Name: Robo Rush
Organizer: NIIT University
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Robo Rush
Build and race your custom-designed bot through a thrilling obstacle course. Speed, stability, and strategy will decide the winner. Every turn, barrier, and hurdle will test the bot’s engineering design and the team’s problem-solving abilities.
Platform:
On-Campus (Offline Event)
Team Size:
3–5 members per team
Overview:
The Bot Racing Challenge is designed to test the creativity, technical skills, and teamwork of participants. Teams must design and construct a wirelessly controlled robot that is capable of navigating a dynamic and obstacle-filled racecourse. The track will include sharp turns, uneven surfaces, wedges, speed breakers, and narrow sections that require precise control and agility.
The primary objective is to complete the course in the shortest possible time. However, it is not just about speed—maneuverability, stability, and strategy play equally important roles. A team that balances careful navigation with quick execution will have the best chance of securing victory.
This competition is not only about winning but also about learning. Participants will get an opportunity to apply engineering concepts, experiment with design ideas, and showcase innovative thinking in real-world problem solving.
Rules & Regulations
Immobility: If the robot is unable to move for three minutes, the team will be disqualified.
Toppling: If a robot topples, it will be placed back at the last successfully cleared checkpoint.
Skipping Obstacles:
Teams may choose to skip an obstacle if their bot is unable to cross it.
Skipping incurs a time penalty of 10–15 seconds, depending on the checkpoint.
Skips are limited to 25% of total checkpoints.
Off-Track: If a bot leaves the racing path, it will be placed at the last cleared checkpoint and allowed to continue.
Shortcuts: Not allowed. Bots must follow the designated track and complete all sections.
Disqualification: Any form of misconduct, tampering with the track, or violation of the above rules will result in immediate disqualification.
Final Authority: In case of disputes, the organizers’ and jury’s decision will be final and binding.
Bot Specifications
Must fit inside a 30cm x 30cm (L x B) box.
Operated via wireless control only.
Powered by an on-board power supply not exceeding 12V DC between any two points.
No use of readymade kits or assemblies is allowed; bots must be custom-built.
Track Specifications
Track width: 0.3m – 0.5m.
Contains U-turns, right-angled turns, wedges, loops, hurdles, speed breakers, and narrow paths.
Designed to test a bot’s stability, maneuverability, and speed in real time.
Judging Criteria & Scoring:
Fastest Completion Time will determine the winner.
Time Penalties:
Skipping obstacles → +10–15 seconds.
Going off-track → restart from the last checkpoint.
Disqualification Cases: immobility, repeated misconduct, use of shortcuts, or exceeding design specifications.
Team Guidelines:
Each team must have 3–5 members, all from the same institution.
Collaboration is key: team members may divide roles into design, programming, control, and strategy.
Teams are encouraged to maintain sportsmanship and respect towards fellow competitors.
Event Flow:
Registration: Teams register on-campus before the deadline.
Qualification Round (if needed): Teams may be shortlisted based on design checks or preliminary runs.
Final Round: Bots race on the complete obstacle course in timed runs.
Winner Declaration: Based on fastest valid completion time after penalties.
This competition promises an exciting mix of engineering, strategy, and adrenaline-pumping action. Whether you race for victory or for the love of robotics, the Bot Racing Challenge guarantees a fun and enriching experience for all participants.

Name: Machine Learning Hackathon
Organizer: Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Bhubaneswar
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Machine Learning Hackathon
This hackathon is designed to challenge participants on their machine learning knowledge and practical skills through two rounds - one online and one offline.
Eligibility:
Open to all college students.
Participants can form teams of up to 4 members.
Round 1: Online Quiz:
All registered participants must attempt an online, time-bound quiz covering core machine learning concepts.
The quiz must be completed in one sitting without external assistance or bots.
The top 30 teams, based on cumulative scores, will qualify for the next round.
Round 2: Offline Hackathon:
Qualified teams (up to 30) will participate in an offline hackathon event.
A common Problem statement will be shared in the presence of all teams.
Teams will have a fixed duration to develop a machine learning project from scratch.
All work must be completed during the hackathon period; pre-existing code is not allowed except for open-source libraries and frameworks.
Teams must submit:
Project code (with clear documentation)
Dataset(s) used
Detailed project report (problem statement, solution approach, model architecture, evaluation metrics, and real-world application)
Project presentation (for final evaluation)
Team Formation:
Teams can have a maximum of 4 members.
Teams must be finalized before the start of Round 2.
Each team must designate a representative for communication with organizers.
Project Guidelines:
All projects must be original and developed during the hackathon.
Use of open-source libraries, frameworks, and public datasets is permitted.
Clearly document any external resources or code used.
Projects must adhere to ethical AI practices and responsible data usage.
Any continuation of previous work must be disclosed, and only new features or improvements will be judged.
Judging Criteria:
Projects will be evaluated based on the following:
Technical Accuracy (50%) – Correctness and robustness of ML methods and implementation.
Innovation & Creativity (20%) – Originality of the idea and solution approach.
Execution & Functionality (15%) – Working prototype, code quality, and usability.
Presentation & Clarity (15%) – Effectiveness of project explanation and visual aids.
Judges will use a rating scale (e.g., 1–10) for each criterion to ensure fairness and consistency.
In case of a tie, the team with stronger technical accuracy will be ranked higher.
Submission Requirements:
Submit all code in a well-documented GitHub repository.
Include a detailed report (PDF/Markdown) covering:
Problem statement
Dataset(s) used
Model architecture
Evaluation metrics
Results and analysis
Prepare a project presentation (slides) for the final demo session.
All submissions must be completed before the announced deadline; late submissions will not be accepted.
Prohibited Activities:
No use of bots, AI assistants, or external help during the quiz or hackathon.
No plagiarism; all work must be original.
No re-use of code written before the hackathon except for open-source libraries/frameworks.
Any violation of rules, code of conduct, or unsporting behavior will result in immediate disqualification at the organizers’ discretion.
Code of Conduct:
All participants must maintain a respectful and inclusive environment.
Harassment, discrimination, or inappropriate behavior will not be tolerated and may result in disqualification.
General Terms:
Organizers reserve the right to modify the rules, disqualify participants, or cancel the event in case of unforeseen circumstances.
All decisions by the judging panel and organizers are final and binding.

Name: Planck'd
Organizer: International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Bangalore
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Planck'd
Planck’d
is a Quantum Computing Hackathon organized by the Quantum Computing Club of IIIT Bangalore, Qimaya.
Stages and Timeline
3-day Online Event
Event Structure
It will be a 3-day online hackathon.
Quantum Algorithms track is based on the implementation of quantum algorithms
QML track will be based on real-world data.
The objective is to build efficient implementations which satisfy the problem description.
Rules
Plagiarism is not allowed
A participant can be part of only 1 team
Teams of maximum 4 members are allowed
Participants can use any quantum framework and implement in any programming language they wish (we prefer Qiskit/Pennylane/Classiq frameworks with Python/C++/Java)
Evaluation Criteria
Quantum Machine Learning Track
Innovation and Uniqueness:
Creativity and originality of your model.
Presentation & Understanding
: Clear explanation of approach, trade-offs, and insights.
Performance Evaluation:
Measure Precision, Recall, F1-score, Accuracy; balance false positives and negatives.
Event Specifications
Number of Participants:
upto 4 member per team
Total Teams Allowed:
No Limit
Eligibility:
Open for All
Format:
Online Event
Type:
Team event
Tracks:
Quantum Algorithms Track
Quantum Machine Learning Track
Prizes (per track):
1st Place - ₹6,000
2nd Place - ₹4,000
Quantum Algorithms Track Topics
The Quantum Algorithms Track typically covers fundamental concepts and key algorithms that demonstrate a quantum advantage over their classical counterparts. Topics include (but are not restricted to):
Qubits and Quantum States: Superposition, Entanglement, Mixed states, Density Matrices, Bloch sphere representation.
Quantum Gates and Circuits: Universal set of gates (Hadamard, Pauli gates, CNOT, Toffoli), circuit model, measurement, and unitarity.

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Vecna's Circuit
Brief Description
Think you can survive the circuit? OWASP PCCOE presents Vecna's Circuit, a multi-round, duo-based technical challenge. Decrypt messages in "The Void," hunt for clues in the "Demogorgon Hunt," and conquer the "Upside Down." Register now and test your skills!
About the Competition
Vecna's Circuit
, an offline technical event designed to challenge students with progressive rounds of cybersecurity problem-solving. This duo-based competition will test your critical thinking, teamwork, and technical skills across diverse fields of cybersecurity.
Participants will journey through three thrilling rounds, starting with cryptographic puzzles and SQL mysteries, advancing to uncover hidden information through OSINT investigations, and culminating in a final test of their Linux command-line skills. This is a practical, competitive, and engaging platform to apply and enhance your abilities in a timed, high-stakes environment.
Competition Structure
The event is a duo-based challenge consisting of three progressive rounds:
Round 1: "The Void"
- Participants will face a series of cryptography and SQL-based mystery challenges.
Round 2: "Demogorgon Hunt"
- This round will test your digital investigation skills through OSINT (Open-Source Intelligence) challenges.
Round 3: "The Upside Down"
- The final round will challenge your proficiency with Linux-Based Command-Line tasks.
Eligibility & Team Rules
The event is open to all Btech students.
Participation is strictly in teams of two (Duo-Based).
Prerequisites
Each participant must bring a laptop with Internet Access.
Pre-installed Tools:
A modern browser (Chrome/Firefox).
Kali or Ubuntu Linux.
Why You Should Participate
Improved logical reasoning and puzzle-solving skills through cryptography and SQL challenges.
Hands-on experience in digital investigation and data gathering with OSINT tasks.
A stronger command-line proficiency and problem-solving skills in Linux environments.
Enhanced teamwork and strategy under the pressure of timed challenges.
Motivation to explore careers in offensive security and cybersecurity.
Event Details & Timeline

October 6, 2025

4:00 PM - 7:00 PM

PCCOE Campus, Akurdi, Pune.

Organizer: GGSIPU USAR ACM Student Chapter
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Silicon Quest : ANIMEVERSE
Join our whatsapp group for detailed notifications :
https://chat.whatsapp.com/J7w50KD3qXTKMpo3E5TUtv
Overview:
Prepare for
Animeverse
, the ultimate battleground where your passion for anime and your prowess in technology will be pushed to the limit.
Hosted by the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM), USAR, this is not just another competition; it's a two-stage gauntlet designed to challenge the most innovative minds.
Assemble your squad, solve cryptic technical challenges in a campus-wide quest, and forge a legendary tech solution in a high-speed hackathon.
This is your arena to prove you are the ultimate tech otaku.
Guidelines:
Eligibility:
Open to all! Students, professionals, hobbyists, and creators are welcome.
If you have a passion for technology, problem-solving, and the world of anime, this event is for you.
Team Size: Each team can consist of 1–3 members.
Inter-college/Inter-company Teams: Allowed and encouraged! Teams can include members from different colleges, companies, or backgrounds.
Inter-specialization Teams: Yes! Diverse teams with a mix of skills such as developers, designers, and strategists are highly encouraged.
Event Format:
The event is divided into two exciting, back-to-back rounds on the same day:
Round 1: The Digital Otaku Quest (Treasure Hunt):
A fun and fast-paced qualifying round where teams solve technical challenges to earn their spot in the final hackathon.
Round 2: The Anime Innovation Forge (Hackathon):
Qualifying teams will participate in a single-day, rapid-prototyping hackathon to build a functional solution for a given problem statement.
Process & Round Details:
Round 1: The Digital Otaku Quest
Objective:
Teams will explore the event area to find and scan various anime-themed QR codes. Each scan reveals a technical question. A correct answer “claims” a digital card for the team. This round is designed to be collaborative and fun.
Process:
Registered teams will gather for a short briefing.
Once the quest begins, teams will be free to find QR codes at their own pace.
Answers are submitted through an online portal.
A live leaderboard will display team progress in real time.
Top-performing teams will qualify for Round 2.
Round 2: The Anime Innovation Forge
Objective:
Qualifying teams will be given a theme related to anime and technology. The challenge is to brainstorm, design, and build a working prototype of a tech-based solution.
Process:
Qualifying teams will attend a briefing where the hackathon theme is announced.
Teams will then begin project development.
Mentors will be available to guide and assist with ideas or technical challenges.
After development, teams will finalize submissions.
Each team will give a short presentation of their project to a panel of judges.
Winners will be announced during the closing ceremony.
Rules & Culture:
The goal is to foster a fun, inclusive, and collaborative environment.
These rules ensure a fair and positive experience for everyone.
Fair Play & Spirit:
Collaborate: This event is about teamwork—work together and have fun.
Respect: All participants must be respectful and supportive. Harassment of any kind will not be tolerated.
Integrity: The organizers’ and judges’ decisions are final. Participants are expected to compete with sportsmanship.
Round 1: The Digital Otaku Quest
Open-Book: Participants may use the internet or any resources to solve problems. The focus is on learning and problem-solving.
Respect the Game: Do not tamper with or remove any QR codes. Maintain fairness for all teams.
Round 2: The Anime Innovation Forge
Build on Your Best: Projects should be primarily built during the hackathon. Use of open-source libraries, APIs, frameworks, and personal code snippets is allowed.
Judging Criteria:
Creativity & Innovation – How unique and exciting is the idea?
Impact – Does the project solve an interesting or meaningful problem?
Execution – How well does the demo work?
Presentation – How effectively does the team communicate their idea?

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Excalibur
About
Excalibur is a competitive software development event designed to challenge and enhance participants' problem-solving, coding, and presentation skills.
This is a strictly Software Development event; projects involving Hardware or IIOT will not be allowed.
The event spans over several weeks and comprises multiple rounds designed to test teams at every stage of development.
Eligibility
Students from the first, second, and pre-final years of all branches and programs are eligible to participate.
Inter-year and inter-college teams are strictly not allowed.
Team Composition
A team can have 4 members.
Each team must include at least one female member.
Participants are not allowed to switch teams after registration.
Event Rounds and Timelines
Registration Ends: 12th October
All interested teams must complete their registration by this date.
Round 1 (Initial PPT Screening): 13th - 14th October
Teams to present their PowerPoint presentations covering the problem statement, proposed solution, architecture, tech stack, timeline, and team responsibilities. All other details will be provided during the event.
Evaluation Criteria:
Idea feasibility, innovation, presentation clarity, and technical approach.
Mentor Assignment: 15th October
Teams that successfully pass the PPT round will be assigned a mentor for guidance.
Round 2 - First Evaluation: 21st - 22nd October
Teams meet with judges for project evaluation. A partial or complete demonstration of the project is mandatory.
Evaluation Criteria:
Project progress, code quality, and adherence to the proposed timeline.
Round 2 Results: 23rd October
Teams qualifying for the next stage will be announced.
Round 3 - Mentor Evaluation: 25th October
A second evaluation will be conducted to assess the progress made since the first round and readiness for the final presentation. Only Mentors will participate in this discussion. So always keep your mentor updated about the progress of your project.
Round 3 Results: 26th October
The teams selected to advance to the Final Round will be announced.
Final Round - Offline Campus Presentation: 28th October
Finalist teams present their completed projects on campus to a panel of judges, including a live demonstration and Q&A session.
Evaluation Criteria:
Final functionality, user experience, innovation, technical complexity, and presentation skills.
General Guidelines
Use of Git is mandatory for version control.
Teams are required to adhere to the given timeline for all submissions and evaluations.
Teams must actively participate in all mentor sessions.
Teams are required to be present on-campus for the final presentation.
Plagiarism or copying of projects will lead to immediate disqualification.
Participants must follow all instructions from event organizers and mentors.
The decision of the judges will be final and binding.
Registration Method
Registrations are open on the Unstop website

Name: Pitchcraft
Organizer: Guru Tegh Bahadur 4th Centenary Engineering College
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Pitchcraft
Innovation thrives where imagination meets execution, and PITCHCRAFT stands as a perfect embodiment of that spirit. Designed as a dynamic blend of Ideathon and Hackathon, this multi-stage competition is more than just a contest—it’s a complete innovation journey that challenges participants to think critically, design creatively, and build practically. The goal is to take a concept from an abstract idea to a tangible, real-world tech solution, fostering the skills essential for the innovators and entrepreneurs of tomorrow.
A Platform for Creative Minds
Open to students from all disciplines and institutions, PITCHCRAFT provides a collaborative environment where technology meets creativity.
With a maximum team size of four members, participants are encouraged to merge diverse skill sets and perspectives.
The event invites problem solvers, developers, designers, and visionaries to work together, simulate real-world problem-solving environments, and learn the essence of teamwork, innovation, and execution.
Three Stages of Innovation: From Concept to Creation
The PITCHCRAFT Challenge is structured around a three-stage Problem-Solution model, designed to progressively evaluate and enhance participants’ innovation potential.
Theme: Smart Automation, Artificial Intelligence Technology, Space Technology, Health or
Technology, Agriculture Solution, Robotic & Drone, Blockchain & Cybersecurity, AR and VR Technology.
Round 1: Idea Submission (Online)
The journey begins with the online idea submission round—an intellectual test of creativity
and clarity. Teams are required to identify a significant problem and propose a tech-driven,
innovative solution within two pages. This stage focuses heavily on problem identification,
originality, and technical feasibility. Judges evaluate entries based on innovation (50%) and
clarity of the problem (30%), ensuring only the most promising ideas advance. Out of all
submissions, the top 25 teams earn the opportunity to move forward.
Round 2: Presentation Submission (Online)
This round is all about communication and strategy. Participants must transform their
abstract concepts into structured plans through a detailed presentation consisting of 6 to
10 slides. The presentation should outline the solution architecture, market analysis,
implementation plan, and a prototype design. Beyond technical expertise, this stage
emphasizes storytelling, visual communication, and market relevance. The judges assess
participants on content depth, clarity, and presentation skills
.
After this rigorous evaluation, 15 teams qualify for the grandfinale.
Round 3: Final Showcase Demo (Offline)
The final showdown brings ideas to life. The shortlisted teams present a working prototype or proof-of-concept (PoC) in an offline showcase. This phase embodies the transformation
from theory to practice—where concepts evolve into functional models. Teams demonstrate
their innovation through a live presentation and demonstration before an expert panel and an
audience. The evaluation in this round prioritizes technical execution, functionality, and
user experience (50%), highlighting both creativity and practical applicability. The winners
are then crowned based on their overall innovation impact and technical excellence.

Organizer: Government Mahila Engineering College Ajmer
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Crack the Code - A Code Scramble Challenge
Organized by WIE Student Branch GWECA 2025. Celebrate IEEE Week with an exciting online coding competition that tests your logic, debugging skills, and programming versatility.
About the Event:
"
Unscramble the chaos, Make the code work"
Crack the Code is an online coding challenge where participants are presented with scrambled code snippets that they must rearrange into correct, executable programs. The event tests problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and logical thinking under time constraints.
Format and Process:
Round 1: Beginner’s Buzz
Questions: 15 simple scrambled code snippets in Python
Focus on basic syntax and logic
All registered participants can attempt this stage
Top 50 participants qualify for Stage 2
Round 2: Intermediate Logic
Questions: 10 moderately challenging scrambled codes in C++
Focus on loops, functions, and conditional logic
Only top 50 participants from Stage 1 are eligible
Top 10 participants advance to the final stage
Round 3: The Final Showdown
Questions: 5 complex problems in Java
Focus on object-oriented programming, data structures, and advanced logic
Determines the Top 3 winners of Crack the Code
Judges’ and organizers’ decisions are final and binding.
Judging Criteria:
Accuracy
: Correct syntax and logical correctness
Speed
: Time taken to submit correct code
Efficiency
: Code readability and optimization
Rules & Guidelines:
Individual participation only; no teams allowed
Solutions can be written in any programming language
Participants must rearrange the given scrambled code correctly according to the language provided for each round
Platform: Unstop (Live)
A stable internet connection is mandatory during all rounds
No external help, AI tools, or unfair means allowed
In case of ties, submission time will be considered
Judges’ and organizers’ decisions are final and binding

Name: Hack36 9.0 Secure
Organizer: Motilal Nehru National Institute of Technology, Allahabad
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Hack36 9.0 Secure
Hack36 is the annual national-level hackathon organized by the Computer Coding Club (CC) of MNNIT Allahabad. It is a 36-hour-long coding marathon where developers, designers, and innovators from across the country come together to solve real-world problems using technology. The event is known for its intense competition, collaborative spirit, and high-quality projects, offering participants a unique chance to learn, build, and network with like-minded individuals.
This year's Hack36 is part of the
Network Security Bootcamp
being organized at MNNIT supported by
ISEA Phase III, Ministry of Electronics and IT
Important! ->
For completion make sure to register for the Bootcamp on the IVP Portal of ISEA (get link from the official website given in banner card of the event)
Why Participate in Hack36?
Hack36 is more than just a hackathon; it’s a launchpad for young developers looking to showcase their skills, collaborate with industry experts, and gain real-world experience. This year we focus on Cybersecurity and its applications across various domains including Network Security, Secure Information Retreival, Security in Artificial Intelligence, Web3 and Web2. Its more like asling yourself, how does my software, my product ensures safety of its users or how does my product solve some security challenge
Opportunities at Hack36:
Internship & Job Offers:
Hack36 provides a golden opportunity for students to connect with top tech companies, startups, and recruiters. Many participants secure internships and full-time job offers based on their performance. Sponsors and industry mentors actively scout for talent, making it an excellent platform for career growth.
Industry Exposure & Mentorship:
Participants get access to mentorship from industry professionals and academicians, helping them refine their ideas and improve their technical skills. Direct interactions with leading engineers, developers, and tech leaders provide insights into industry trends and best practices.
Networking & Community Building:
Hack36 is a hub for networking with fellow coders, designers, and tech enthusiasts. It is a great opportunity to form connections, collaborate on future projects, and become part of the larger developer community. Many participants find long-term teammates for future hackathons and open-source projects.
Hands-on Experience with Latest Technologies:
Participants gain practical exposure to cutting-edge tools, APIs, and cloud platforms provided by sponsors like Google, GitHub, Microsoft, and Amazon. This hands-on experience boosts their technical knowledge and problem-solving abilities.
Prizes, Recognition & Post-Hackathon Support:
Winning teams receive cash prizes, swags, and startup incubation opportunities. Some projects also get continued support in the form of funding, technical assistance, and mentorship, allowing them to grow beyond the hackathon.
Structure of the Event:
The event spans 36 hours, divided into key phases:
Registration & Selection:
Teams (2-4 members) are shortlisted based on their problem-solving approach, resume, past projects, experience and achievements.
Hackathon Kickoff:
Opening ceremony, start of development time, and mentorship sessions.
Development & Mid-Evaluation:
Teams build their prototypes with guidance from mentors.
Final Submission & Judging:
Projects are evaluated on innovation, technical complexity, and impact.
Demo & Awards:
Top teams present their solutions, winners are announced and all top teams receive their respective rewards
Conclusion
Hack36 is a life-changing experience for tech enthusiasts, providing them with career-defining opportunities, industry exposure, and a platform to innovate. Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced coder, Hack36 is the perfect place to showcase your talent, learn from peers, and build something impactful.

Problem Statements:
Participants can choose any one of the following tracks and problem statements to ideate and submit innovative solutions.
Track 1: Education & Learning Innovation
AI-Powered Personalized Learning Companion
Problem: Students have unique learning speeds and difficulties, but e-learning platforms fail to adapt in real time.
Challenge: Build an AI-based adaptive learning system that analyzes behavior (quiz responses, hesitation time, engagement) and personalizes lessons, difficulty, and recommendations.
Academic Integrity & AI Plagiarism Detection
Problem: Generative AI tools blur the line between original and AI-generated writing.
Challenge: Create an AI engine that identifies AI-generated or paraphrased content, flags misuse, and promotes ethical academic practices.
Track 2: Healthcare & Wellness
Predictive Health Risk Analyzer
Problem: Diseases like diabetes or hypertension go unnoticed until severe stages.
Challenge: Design an AI model that predicts health risks using user data such as habits, wearable data, and medical history.
Mental Health & Emotional Wellbeing Bot
Problem: Millions face stress or anxiety, but access to care is limited and stigmatized.
Challenge: Build an AI chatbot that detects emotional distress, tracks mood, and offers personalized self-care or coping guidance.
Track 3: Revolutionizing Food Delivery
Smart Route & Freshness Optimization System
Problem: Delivery delays due to traffic or routing cause cold food and dissatisfaction.
Challenge: Create an AI engine that predicts delays and dynamically adjusts delivery routes using real-time GPS and traffic data.
Food Waste Reduction Using Predictive AI
Problem: Restaurants waste food due to uncertain demand and overproduction.
Challenge: Build an AI-powered forecasting tool that predicts daily order volume and suggests redistribution of surplus food.
Track 4: Smart Agriculture & Sustainability
Precision Farming Assistant
Problem: Farmers rely on guesswork for irrigation and pesticide use, wasting resources.
Challenge: Develop an IoT + AI platform that uses soil, weather, and moisture data to optimize irrigation, fertilizer, and pest control.
Crop Disease Detection & Yield Prediction
Problem: Crop diseases destroy yields before detection, and yield prediction is unreliable.
Challenge: Create a vision-based AI tool that detects crop diseases from images and forecasts yield using environmental data.
Track 5: IoT & Cross-Industry Innovation
Smart Energy & Asset Management System
Problem: Industries and campuses waste energy due to poor monitoring.
Challenge: Design an IoT-integrated system that tracks real-time energy use, predicts failures, and automates energy-saving actions.
Track 6: Cybersecurity & Data Protection
AI-Driven Adaptive Threat Detection
Problem: Cyberattacks evolve faster than static firewalls can respond.
Challenge: Develop an AI agent that learns from new attack patterns, analyzes network behavior, and adapts security measures in real time.
Track 7: AI & FinTech Innovation
AI-Based Credit Scoring for Financial Inclusion
Problem: Traditional credit scoring excludes people without formal banking history.
Challenge: Create an AI model that uses alternative data (mobile payments, bills, spending habits) to compute fair, dynamic credit scores.
Fraud Detection & Transaction Anomaly Engine
Problem: Fraudsters exploit static systems to hide suspicious transactions.
Challenge: Design an AI engine that analyzes user behavior and transaction patterns to detect and prevent fraud in real time.
Optional: Open Innovation Track
Participants may propose any AI-driven solution addressing a major social or environmental challenge, such as waste management, disaster prediction, or digital accessibility.

Name: Capture The Flag (CTF)
Organizer: International Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Bhubaneswar
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Capture The Flag (CTF)
About Capture The Flag (CTF):
A Capture The Flag (CTF) competition is a cybersecurity-based contest where participants solve a variety of real-world hacking challenges to ﬁnd hidden clues called ﬂags. These ﬂags act as proof of solving a challenge and are submitted on a CTF platform to earn points.
Each challenge represents a practical security scenario, encouraging participants to think like ethical hackers while maintaining legal and responsible boundaries. The aim is not just to break systems but to understand, analyze, and defend them that promote both offensive and defensive cybersecurity skills.
About the Event:
The Capture the Flag (CTF) competition hosted by Tech Society IIIT Bhubaneswar is a cybersecurity challenge designed to test your hacking, analytical, and problem-solving skills in a real-world simulation environment.
In this event, participants will face a series of challenges related to ethical hacking domains that include web exploitation, cryptography, forensics, reverse engineering, OSINT (Open-Source Intelligence), steganography, and privilege escalation.
Each challenge will test different aspects of cybersecurity knowledge and technical expertise. The event is meant to promote cybersecurity awareness, encourage ethical hacking practices, and create a platform for collaboration among students passionate about security.
This event will be conducted offline at IIIT Bhubaneswar, giving participants a chance to interact with cybersecurity enthusiasts, mentors, and experts from different colleges.
Rules and Guidelines:
Preliminary Round:
Open to all colleges across Odisha and nearby states.
Details about the internal prelims will be shared through e-mail.
Eligibility:
Open to all students currently pursuing bachelor's and master's degrees.
No student can participate in multiple teams.
CTF participants are allowed to participate in all other events except those occurring parallel to CTF.
Team Formation:
Individual participation is not allowed.
Teams can consist of 2 to 3 members.
Participants can form their own teams.
Teams may include members from different colleges, as long as they are from the same state.
Duration:
The event will run for 8 hours and will be hosted at IIIT Bhubaneswar.
Event Mode:
The competition will be hosted on a dedicated server, and all challenges must be solved there.
Update:
We are introducing a preliminary PPT round for CTF. Submit fresh and unique projects focused on Cybersecurity to advance in the competition.
Submission Deadline: 04.11.2025
Please check your registered mail for detailed guidelines and submission instructions.
Code of Conduct:
All participants must create a safe, respectful, and inclusive environment.
Discrimination, harassment, or any form of disrespectful behavior will not be tolerated and may result in disqualiﬁcation.
Participants must not attack the competition infrastructure or not engage in any form of unethical behavior.
Any form of brute-force attacks on the platform (including login attempts) will lead to disqualiﬁcation.
Submission Requirements:
All the required information asked to be gathered needs to be submitted within the given timeframe of 8 hours. (10pm-6am)
Hints will not be given by the judges , it will be provided on the dedicated CTF platform.
A google form will be provided for the submission of the asked information, also mentioning what info related to the given situation is to be submitted.
Flags must be submitted on the CTF platform. Partial or incorrect ﬂags will not be accepted.
Any help regarding questions we will help.
Technology Stack:
Participants are free to use any programming languages, frameworks, and tools to complete the given challenges.
VM – kali Linux (recommended).
Participants must rely on their skills, documentation, and publicly available non AI assisted tools.
The use of AI-based tools (like ChatGPT, Copilot, Bard, etc.) is strictly prohibited.
Disqualiﬁcation Criteria:
Use of AI tools to generate solutions.
Attempting to attack the event’s server beyond the intended scope.
Sharing answers or collaborating with other teams.
Misbehaving with other team members or misleading judges.
No one should leave the event venue.
Violation of any of the above rules.
Judging Criteria:
The team with most no. of correct answers to the questions asked and total no of points calculated before 5 am will be preferred and in case of tie, faster submissions will be given the upper hand.
Judges’ decisions will be ﬁnal.
General Instructions:
Participants should bring their own devices and necessary equipment like charger, electric board etc.
No plagiarism devices or any short of utility will be provided or allowed.

Cozmoclench  @Techfest IIT Bombay x Amalthea IIT Gandhinagar
Cozmoclench is a flagship robotics competition of Techfest, IIT Bombay, where innovation meets precision. Participants design and build manually controlled bots capable of gripping, transporting, and depositing objects while navigating a 3000 mm × 3000 mm arena packed with challenging obstacles.
From ramps, pits, and bridges to precision deposit zones, every element of the arena is designed to test a bot’s agility, control, and engineering design. The challenge goes beyond raw mechanics; t demands strategic thinking, adaptive control, and flawless execution under time pressure. With separate paths for wired and wireless bots, the event ensures both fairness and complexity, making Cozmoclench a true test of robotics skill.

Name: Data Analytics
Organizer: Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Kharagpur
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Data Analytics
Jets are streams of particles produced in high-energy collisions, such as those at the Large Hadron Collider (LHC). They provide vital insight into the fundamental forces of nature and can reveal rare processes or new particles beyond the Standard Model. Studying jets is essential not only for understanding known physics but also for discovering anomalies that may signal new phenomena. Unusual jets could point to exotic processes, detector effects, or entirely new physics.
Submission Guidelines:
The Problem Statement is only visible after registering.
This is an online submission round. Teams are required to submit a well-structured solution answering the specified case questions.
The submission should demonstrate clarity, depth of analysis, logical structure, use of evidence, and relevance to the case.
Participants may use any programming language or library (e.g., Python with TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn). However, it is preferable to use Google Colab or Jupyter Notebook as the coding environment.
Compile all the files in a structured manner and upload them on a drive folder. The drive link should be submitted through unstop. Ensure that the viewer's access is granted.
Submissions should include:
○ A well-documented .ipynb notebook with code and outputs.
○ A PDF report containing detailed explanations, plots, tables, and results. All figures should be clearly labelled, and final answers should be highlighted.
File Type: Google Drive link
Deadline: 14th October 2025
Rules and Regulations:
This is a team event which is open to college students only.
After registering on NSSC's website, the team leader should register their team for the event on Unstop as well.
Each team can have a maximum of 4 participants. The members of the team can be from different colleges.
This is an event comprising two rounds. For the first round, a problem statement will be released. Each team can submit multiple entries, and in that case only the latest entry will be considered.
The top three teams will be awarded certificates of excellence.
The teams invited for the offline round will receive a participation certificate.

nssc.in
:

. Each participant will get a participant ID in the format 25-xxxxxx (25 followed by 6 digits). Please note that this does
NOT
constitute the team ID. Participant IDs of all concerned participants have to be submitted on Unstop, separated by commas.
Go to Data Analytics (
nssc.in/events/dataanalytics
) and register as a team (if you haven't already). Once your team's registration is confirmed, your team will be assigned a team ID in the format T-xxxxxxx (T followed by 7 digits). This will be the ID associated with your team for the rest of the event, and this is the ID you need to enter under "Team ID" during registration on Unstop.
Please note that
entering your team ID on Unstop is mandatory to be considered for the further selection process
. Otherwise, you won't be eligible to qualify for the next rounds, irrespective of your performance in this round.

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Startup-Hackathon 2.0 - Self Employing Youth of India for A Better Future
Do you harbor the passion for entrepreneurship but struggle with ideation paralysis or the minimum viable product (MVP) gap? Stop waiting for the perfect moment. This isn't just a competition; it's your definitive on-ramp into the high-octane world of venture creation. This event eliminates the initial friction by providing the opportunity set—you supply the execution prowess.
Event & Eligibility: Your Gateway to Disruption
This immersive, high-velocity Startup Hackathon is your chance to pivot from aspiring enthusiast to founder-in-motion.
Date of Deployment: Sunday, October 19th , starting at 9:00 AM sharp.
Team Configuration: Form your A-team with 1–4 members (a minimum of one dedicated coder/developer is mandatory for technical viability).
Participant Spectrum: Open to all innovators, from university students seeking experiential learning to working professionals aiming for a career pivot. Cross-functional and cross-institutional teams are highly encouraged.
The Competition Structure: Three Phases of Execution (Online):
The entire hackathon will be conducted online and structured into three intense, time-boxed phases designed to mimic real-world startup sprints:
Phase 1: Idea Selection (1 Hour): Every team will be granted exclusive access to a curated portfolio of 25+ pre-vetted startup ideas. Your objective is to perform rapid concept validation and select the single idea with the highest potential for market disruption.
Phase 2: Prototype Development (5 Hours): Utilizing the concept selected, your team must immediately transmute the idea into a functional, working prototype. This sprint emphasizes leveraging modern, low-code/no-code tools for speed and agility.
Phase 3: PMF Validation (1 Hour 30 Minutes): The final phase focuses on Product-Market Fit (PMF). Teams will be challenged to test and validate their Go-To-Market strategies using the prototype and prepare a compelling pitch based on initial traction data.
The Value Proposition & Recognition:
We've designed this hackathon to be a concentrated dose of the startup lifecycle and reward future potential:
Founder Mastery: Acquire essential skills in end-to-end venture building, from initial concept validation to developing a compelling investor-ready pitch deck.
Expert Vetting: Present your final solution to a panel of expert judges, mimicking the critical seed-stage pitch experience.
Exciting Prizes: Winners will receive prizes worth ₹5000 in the form of goodies and invaluable mentorship opportunities from our network of seasoned entrepreneurs and industry experts.
Incentivized Scaling: Top-tier teams secure automatic entry and 2-Month Free Access to the highly coveted School of Entrepreneurship, an exclusive founder incubation pipeline.
Validation: All participants will receive a formal Certificate of Participation, confirming your engagement in this intense disruptor crucible.

About Content:
Everything you need to know about
Silicon Maze
Silicon Maze
is a 24 hour student-run hackathon organized in hybrid mode by Web Enthusiasts' Club NITK, Surathkal. This is the 6th edition with the theme revolving around
SpiderVerse.
It is a platform for first and second-year undergraduate students to showcase their skills, ideas and potential in various disciplines of Computer Science like Data Structures and Algorithms, Systems, Machine Learning and Development.
Guidelines
:
Discord
Eligibility: Open for
first and second-year
undergraduate students.
Inter-college team members are allowed.
Inter-specialization team members are allowed.
Inter-batch team members are allowed.
A team can have a maximum of 3 people.
It is a 24-hour-long hybrid event - Only NITK students can participate in person at CCC NITK, the rest can participate from the comfort of their homes.
All the participants are requested to join the discord server to ensure smooth communication.
Rules
:
There are 4 tracks - Algorithms, Machine Learning, Systems, and Development.
Algorithms
: Participants will face a series of algorithmic challenges of varying difficulty, designed to test their ability to solve problems using optimal and efficient code. Be ready for intense competition as you tackle complex puzzles related to Spider world scenarios.
Machine
Learning
: The competition will feature a series of tasks that will challenge contestants to demonstrate their proficiency in various areas of the field, such as Generative AI, Exploratory Data Analysis, hyperparameter tuning, and architecture building.
Systems
: Participants will face an exciting array of challenges testing knowledge in infosec, networking, cryptography, and web application security, covering a wide range of topics.
Development
: Contestants will embark on an exhilarating journey through a diverse range of web technologies and experiment with APIs while mastering collaboration via Git. They will have the chance to dive into the world of real-time communication using WebSockets, revamp lacklustre UIs, and explore many more exciting technologies. Gear up to leverage these cutting-edge tools to solve engaging problems.
All the tracks have equal weightage - it is not necessary to attempt every track.
Each question from within a track has a set of points associated with it.
Each question will be centred around the theme of
SpiderVerse
.️️
Participants have 24 hours to solve as many problems from across the tracks.
Top teams with
maximum
points
win!
Results along with the editorials will be released some time after the event.
Evaluation:
Each track will be evaluated independently based on predefined criteria, and the final leaderboard will be computed from cumulative points across all attempted tracks.
The
difficulty
and
innovation
of solutions will carry significant weight, solving harder or more creative problems will earn higher points.
Code quality
and
readability
will be considered; efficient, modular, and well-documented code will be rewarded.
For
Machine Learning
and
Development
tracks, additional emphasis will be given to:
Novelty of approach
and originality in problem-solving
Performance metrics
or output accuracy
Documentation
if applicable
For
Systems
and
Algorithms
tracks, focus areas include:
Efficiency
and
optimality
of solutions
Correctness
and
completeness
of implementations
Decision made by the organizers is final, and cannot be changed
Prize Pool: ₹50,000+
Awesome goodies and Swag
Offline participants get FREE PIZZAS!
With great code comes great responsibility - swing into the Silicon Maze and unleash your inner Spider-Dev!
