Name: ML Challenge - Convergence2K25R
Organizer: Vallurupalli Nageswara Rao Vignana Jyothi Institute of Engineering & Technology, Telangana
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
ML Challenge - Convergence2k25r
Convergence2K25R – ML Challenge:
VNR VJIET proudly presents the Convergence2K25R ML Challenge, a flagship national-level competition designed to inspire innovation, analytical thinking, and technical excellence.
This event brings together students and aspiring ML professionals to apply their skills to meaningful problems, explore new techniques, and develop robust predictive solutions.
You will need a Convergence Pass to attend the events under Convergence 2k25r and You can get your pass at https://rzp.io/rzp/go6vEQcw
Venue:
VNR VJIET, Hyderabad
Event Dates:
3rd & 4th November 2025
About the Challenge:
The challenge invites participants to demonstrate their expertise by tackling a defined problem statement using a provided dataset.
Competitors will analyze data, build predictive models, and optimize their solutions to achieve the highest performance on the evaluation metrics.
Submissions will be judged based on accuracy, methodology, explainability, and overall innovation in approach.
Participation Details:
Team Size: 1–3 members
Round 1: 3rd November (9:00 AM – 5:00 PM)
Final Round: 4th November (9:00 AM – 2:00 PM)
Process:
Problem Release:
The dataset and problem statement will be shared on 3rd November at 9:00 AM at the venue.
Model Development:
Participants can use any ML or DL framework (such as Python, R, TensorFlow, or PyTorch) to preprocess data, train models, and fine-tune performance.
Refinement Phase:
Teams can continue improving their models post the venue session and refine their submissions before the final deadline.
Submission:
Final predictions, code files, and a brief report must be submitted by 4th November at 2:00 PM for evaluation.
Highlights:
Engage in a practical, hands-on ML competition simulating real-world challenges.
Work with curated datasets that encourage analytical depth and problem-solving.
Compete for exciting prizes and recognition for outstanding technical excellence.
Rules and Guidelines:
Participants may compete individually or as a team of up to three members.
Any open-source ML or DL framework can be utilized. All submissions must be original; plagiarism or use of pre-trained public solutions will lead to disqualification.
The dataset and problem statement will be disclosed only at the event venue.
Participants must adhere to the submission timelines and evaluation criteria provided by the organizers. All decisions made by the judging panel will be final and binding.
Submissions will be evaluated based on the performance metric defined in the problem statement, along with the clarity, explainability, and innovation demonstrated in the solution.
Accommodation and food will not be provided.
Join our official
WhatsApp Community
to stay updated with all announcements, discussions, and event details.
Get ready to showcase your data-driven creativity and technical expertise at
Convergence2K25R ML Challenge
. This is your chance to test your limits, collaborate with brilliant minds, and make your mark in the ML landscape.
Think. Train. Predict. Win.

Name: IITK Hackathon
Organizer: Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Kanpur
About Content:
Everything you need to know about
IITK Hackathon
Guidelines:
Eligibility:
Open to all undergraduate and postgraduate students from any recognized college or university across India.
Team Composition:
Team size: 1 to 4 members
Inter-college team members: Allowed
Inter-specialization team members: Allowed
Format & Process:
Round 1:
Online Idea Submission – Teams must submit a detailed abstract of their proposed solution.
Round 2:
Prototype Development – Shortlisted teams will work on developing a working prototype.
Round 3:
Final Presentation – Selected teams will present their projects virtually before the judging panel.
Duration:
The hackathon will be conducted online over multiple days, with each round having specific timelines:
Idea submission: 2 days
Prototype development: 3 days
Final presentation: 1 day
Problem Statements:
Multiple problem statements will be provided across various domains. Each team can choose one statement to work on.
Rules:
Team size must be between 1 and 4 members.
A participant can be part of only one team.
All submissions must be original and created during the hackathon period.
Plagiarism or use of pre-built projects will result in disqualification.
Internet connectivity and necessary setup are the responsibility of participants.
Teams must submit their solutions before the specified deadline for each round.
Decisions made by the judges and organizing committee will be final and binding.
The hackathon will be conducted entirely online through designated platforms.
Problem Statement:
“Propose an Innovative Technology-Driven Solution to a Real-World Problem.”
Description:
The goal of this challenge is to encourage participants to think creatively and develop
practical, technology-based solutions
that can address real-world challenges faced by individuals, communities, or industries.
Participants must identify a
pressing issue
in any domain of their choice — such as
healthcare, education, environment, transportation, agriculture, sustainability, governance, or social welfare
— and propose a
feasible, innovative, and impactful
solution leveraging technology.
Your proposed idea should demonstrate how technology can be used not just for convenience but as a
powerful tool for positive change
— improving quality of life, enhancing efficiency, reducing costs, or promoting inclusivity and accessibility.
Objective:
To inspire innovative thinking among participants by encouraging them to apply their
technical knowledge, creativity, and problem-solving skills
to design sustainable and scalable solutions that have the potential for real-world implementation.
Detailed Expectations:
Your submission should clearly address the following aspects:
Problem Identification:
Describe the problem in detail.
Explain its relevance and why it needs to be solved.
Provide data, examples, or evidence to support the problem’s significance.
Proposed Solution:
Explain your idea clearly and concisely.
Describe how technology will be used to solve the problem.
Mention the innovation or uniqueness in your approach.
Technology Implementation:
Outline the tools, frameworks, or technologies you plan to use (e.g., AI, IoT, Blockchain, Cloud, Data Analytics, AR/VR, Mobile App, Web Platform).
Highlight the technical feasibility and scalability of the solution.
Impact Analysis:
Discuss how your idea will benefit users or society.
Mention potential outcomes like cost reduction, time savings, improved access, or sustainability benefits.
Future Scope:
Explain how your idea can be further developed, expanded, or adapted for broader use.
Expected Deliverable:
A
PowerPoint presentation (PPT)
consisting of
5–8 slides
that outlines your idea, problem background, proposed solution, technology used, and expected impact.
Evaluation Parameters:
Innovation & Creativity
– Originality and uniqueness of the idea.
Feasibility & Practicality
– Can the idea be realistically implemented?
Impact & Scalability
– Social, economic, or environmental benefit and scope for expansion.
Technical Depth
– Use of relevant and effective technology.
Clarity of Presentation
– How well the idea is communicated through the PPT.
